program_name: Property-level self-exclusion / self-limitation (no statewide land-based list)
administering_body: Individual licensed gaming establishments (overseen by Nevada Gaming Control Board)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;Sports betting (retail & mobile);Horse/parimutuel racing (race books);Charitable gaming
enrollment_method: Apply individually at each casino/property; Nev. Gaming Commission Reg. 5.170 requires licensees engaged in credit issuance/check-cashing/direct-mail marketing to let patrons self-limit those privileges
notes: Nevada has NO statewide self-exclusion list for land-based casino, sportsbook, or race book gambling; exclusion is handled property-by-property by each licensee. Reg. 5.170 mandates only patron 'self-limitation' of credit/check-cashing/direct-marketing, not a gambling ban. Statewide self-exclusion exists only for interactive gaming (separate row).

program_name: Statewide self-exclusion list for interactive gaming (Reg. 5A)
enrollment_method: Complete self-exclusion form (online or via a licensed interactive gaming operator); operator submits the form to the Board which adds the person to the statewide list
notes: Nevada Gaming Commission Reg. 5A directs the Board to establish and maintain a statewide list of persons self-excluded from interactive gaming. Online poker is the only authorized interactive gaming in NV. Does NOT apply to land-based gaming. Duration/removal terms need human verification against current Reg. 5A text.

program_name: New Jersey Self-Exclusion Program (DGE Voluntary Self-Exclusion)
administering_body: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement (DGE)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (Atlantic City);iGaming/online casino;Sports betting (retail at AC casinos & racetracks and online)
enrollment_method: Four methods: (1) in person or video conference (covers all casino + sports wagering + internet gaming); (2)/(3) online via DGE portal; (4) via online player account (internet gaming incl. sports wagering). DGE Self-Exclusion Hotline 1-833-788-4DGE
duration_options: One year, five years, or lifetime (full casino exclusion); one or five years (internet-only)
removal_process: Request for Removal from Voluntary Self-Exclusion List (Form 71) after the minimum period expires; lifetime exclusions are permanent
notes: Program established 2001 (Atlantic City casinos), expanded 2013 to internet gaming and 2018 to sports wagering. Single DGE list covers casino, iGaming, and sports wagering with selectable scope (all gaming vs internet-only). Administered with the Council on Compulsive Gambling of NJ.

program_name: Operator-level self-exclusion for fantasy sports (Fantasy Sports Activities Act)
administering_body: Licensed fantasy sports operators (Division of Consumer Affairs - Fantasy Sports Unit)
enrollment_method: Self-exclude with each licensed fantasy sports operator; operators must provide a self-exclusion mechanism under N.J.S.A. 5:20 and implementing regulations
notes: NJ Fantasy Sports Activities Act (P.L. 2017 c.231) requires operators to offer self-exclusion; there is no single statewide DFS self-exclusion list. Enrollment is per-operator.

program_name: Responsible Gaming Database (RGD)
enrollment_method: Online application submitted to the MGCB; written confirmation emailed (can take up to 45 days)
duration_options: 1 year or 5 years (online gaming only / online sports betting only / both)
removal_process: Remains until the selected term expires; reinstatement after term
notes: The RGD lets a patron voluntarily self-exclude from all Michigan-regulated internet gaming and/or internet sports betting. Separate from the Detroit-casino Disassociated Persons List.

program_name: Disassociated Persons List (DPL)
enrollment_method: Submit Disassociated Persons List form via MGCB; processed by MGCB
duration_options: Effectively lifetime; minimum 5 years on the list before a removal request may be filed
removal_process: DPL Request for Removal Application may be filed only after at least 5 years on the list
notes: The DPL is the self-exclusion option for the three Detroit commercial casinos. Tribal casinos run separate property/tribal exclusion programs not covered by the DPL.

program_name: Michigan Lottery Self-Exclusion
administering_body: Michigan Lottery (Bureau of State Lottery; Dept. of Treasury)
enrollment_method: Set via 'Responsible Gaming Tools' in the My Account section of the Michigan Lottery online platform or through the Michigan Lottery Support Center
duration_options: Player-selected period (account closed for that period)
removal_process: Account cannot be reopened for any reason during the self-exclusion period
notes: Applies to the Michigan Lottery online (iLottery) account. NCPG iCAP-certified responsible gaming program.

program_name: National Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program (NVSEP)
administering_body: idPair (private operator); recognized by participating gaming operators in Michigan
verticals_covered: DFS / fantasy sports;Sports betting (online, participating operators);iGaming/online casino (participating operators);poker;Horse/parimutuel racing (participating operators)
enrollment_method: Free registration via the NVSEP platform (nvsep.org); free remote online notary provided where a notarized form is required; a single registration excludes the person across participating multi-state operators
notes: Michigan was one of four NVSEP launch jurisdictions (Aug 2024: Colorado, Iowa, Michigan, Tennessee). NVSEP is operated by idPair and supplements (does not replace) Michigan's state-run RGD and DPL. Coverage spans participating casinos, ~35 online operators, fantasy sports, horse racing, and poker; exact MI operator scope and removal terms need human verification.

program_name: Wind Creek Birmingham — Property-Level Self-Exclusion (Birmingham Race Course)
administering_body: Wind Creek Hospitality (Poarch Band of Creek Indians)
enrollment_method: In-person at property (player tracking database enrollment)
duration_options: Not publicly specified — player tracking DB covers all Wind Creek domestic properties
removal_process: Removal not publicly specified; contact property
notes: No statewide AL racing commission. Birmingham Race Course acquired by Wind Creek Hospitality (Poarch Creek Indians) in April 2025. Wind Creek maintains a universal ban/voluntary self-exclusion in its player-tracking database covering all domestic properties. No Alabama state-level self-exclusion list exists for racing. Program is property/operator-level only.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — county/local-only regulation)
administering_body: N/A (county sheriff or chief of police per county)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Alabama charitable gaming (bingo) is authorized only by individual county constitutional amendments and regulated by the county sheriff or chief of police. No statewide oversight body exists. No self-exclusion program found at state or county level. Official county bingo statutes contain no self-exclusion provisions.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — charitable gaming only)
administering_body: Alaska Department of Revenue Tax Division
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Alaska charitable gaming is regulated by the AK Dept. of Revenue Tax Division under AS 05.15 and 15 AAC 160. Review of statute booklet and official overview documents found no self-exclusion program. Alaska has no state-funded responsible gambling program. No self-exclusion provisions exist for bingo/pull-tab/raffle operators under state law.

program_name: Arizona Department of Gaming Self-Exclusion Program - Tribal Gaming
enrollment_method: In-person at ADG office (notarized form + photo by mail or in-person; notary available at ADG)
duration_options: One year, five years, or ten years
removal_process: No removal process specified (irrevocable for duration of period)
notes: Program known as 'Take Back the Game'. Statewide exclusion covers all Class III Tribal Gaming Facilities in AZ. As of 2026 approximately 2500 active exclusions. Administering body is Arizona Dept. of Gaming at 100 N. 15th Ave Suite 202 Phoenix AZ 85007; contact selfexclusion@azgaming.gov or 602-771-4263. Lottery has separate responsible play tools (az.lotto.com) but no standalone exclusion list found. CHRB not applicable - AZ has no licensed horse tracks separate from tribal gaming.

program_name: Arizona Department of Gaming Self-Exclusion Program - Event Wagering and Fantasy Sports
enrollment_method: In-person at ADG office (notarized form + photo by mail or in-person; notary available at ADG)
duration_options: One year, five years, or ten years
removal_process: No removal process specified (irrevocable for duration of period)
notes: Statewide exclusion covers all licensed Event Wagering (sports betting) and Fantasy Sports Contests in AZ under ADG regulatory purview. Both retail and online sports wagering covered. Same ADG program as tribal gaming exclusion - individuals may choose to exclude from one or both categories.

program_name: Arizona Lottery iLottery Responsible Play - Self-Exclusion (az.lotto.com)
enrollment_method: Online via player account
duration_options: Cool-Off: 72 hours to 3 months; Self-Exclude: 6 months to 1 year
notes: AZ Lottery online/iLottery platform (az.lotto.com) offers account-based self-exclusion (6 months to 1 year) and cool-off tools. This is a separate digital tool from the ADG statewide exclusion program. Retail lottery ticket purchases not covered. No evidence of NVSEP involvement in AZ lottery.

program_name: Arkansas Casino Self-Exclusion (property-by-property; statewide list pending)
administering_body: Arkansas Racing Commission (DFA); individual casino operators (Saracen Casino Resort; Southland Casino Hotel; Oaklawn Racing Casino Resort)
enrollment_method: Per-property: in-person at Security/casino desk or mail (Southland); in-person or online (Saracen)
duration_options: Southland: 2 years; Saracen: player-specified duration; Oaklawn: not publicly specified
removal_process: Per-property request; no statewide reinstatement process yet
notes: Arkansas Racing Commission rule 23 CAR §358-514 requires all casino licensees to maintain self-exclusion registers and prevent self-excluded individuals from playing. Act 879 of 2025 mandates a statewide self-exclusion list; the ARC drafted an amendment to §358-514 but as of May 2026 the rule is still in internal DFA review and has not been forwarded to the Governor. Currently each casino operates its own property-level program. Southland: 2-year term. Saracen: player-specified duration. All three casinos confirmed to have individual programs.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — Arkansas Scholarship Lottery)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Official Arkansas Scholarship Lottery FAQ and Players Hub reviewed. No self-exclusion or voluntary exclusion program exists for lottery players. Responsible gaming page refers only to external resources (NCPG helpline 1-800-522-4700; Gamblers Anonymous). Lottery has NASPL/NCPG Responsible Gambling Certification (2019) but no account-level self-exclusion tool.

program_name: California Bureau of Gambling Control Statewide Self-Exclusion Program (Cardrooms)
administering_body: California Department of Justice - Bureau of Gambling Control
enrollment_method: In-person at any licensed cardroom; or mail to Bureau of Gambling Control / CGCC
removal_process: Submit Self-Exclusion Removal Request form (CGCC-CH7-06) after minimum 1-year minimum term; lifetime terms require min. 1 year on list before removal request
notes: Bureau of Gambling Control administers confidential statewide Self-Exclusion list for licensed cardrooms. NVSEP (idPair) provides free assistance for Californians enrolling in this program since August 2024; California was 5th state to join NVSEP. NVSEP has no jurisdiction over tribal casinos. Program does NOT cover tribal gaming, horse racing, or lottery. Forms: CGCC-CH7-05 (enrollment) and CGCC-CH7-06 (removal request).

program_name: NVSEP - California Card Room Self-Exclusion Assistance
administering_body: idPair / NVSEP (assists with California Bureau of Gambling Control program)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org (with free notarization via partner Proof)
duration_options: Same as state program (one year or lifetime)
notes: idPair/NVSEP launched in California in August 2024. NVSEP assists consumers with enrollment in the state's cardroom self-exclusion program - it does not replace the state program. Partnered with California Council on Problem Gambling. Covers 60+ cardrooms. No jurisdiction over tribal casinos. Does not extend to online/iGaming, sports betting, DFS, lottery, or horse racing (those verticals not legal or separately regulated in CA).

program_name: Operator/Account-Level Self-Exclusion — ADW (no CHRB central list)
administering_body: Individual ADW operators (TVG/FanDuel Racing; TwinSpires; Xpressbet; others licensed by CHRB)
enrollment_method: Account-level via operator website or app (TVG/FanDuel: in-app under Responsible Gaming; TwinSpires: Player Services 877-774-7371 or online)
duration_options: TVG/FanDuel: 3;6;9;12 months; TwinSpires: permanent (others not specified)
removal_process: TVG/FanDuel: automatic end of period; TwinSpires: contact Player Services for permanent removal
notes: CHRB licenses ADW operators but does not maintain a central ADW self-exclusion list. California DOJ self-exclusion program (Bureau of Gambling Control) explicitly does NOT cover horse racing venues. Individual licensed ADW operators (TVG/FanDuel Racing; TwinSpires; Xpressbet) each offer account-level self-exclusion. TVG/FanDuel offers 3/6/9/12-month periods via app. TwinSpires offers permanent exclusion via Player Services call. Program is operator-level only with no CHRB-administered central list.

program_name: Colorado Division of Gaming Self-Exclusion Program (Casinos and Sports Betting)
administering_body: Colorado Division of Gaming / Colorado Limited Gaming Control Commission
enrollment_method: Online (Google form or PDF); in-person or mail to Division of Gaming office; PDF printable
duration_options: One year, three years, or five years
removal_process: Request for removal after minimum period expires (no reinstatement before minimum ends); winnings forfeited
notes: Program launched September 2023. Covers all casinos and sports betting (retail and online) under CLGCC/Division of Gaming purview. Under Rules 29 (limited gaming) and Sports Betting Rule 9. As of Sept 2024 nearly 300 enrollees. Colorado is one of the 4 launch states for NVSEP (August 2024); NVSEP assists with enrollment via nvsep.org/colorado.

program_name: NVSEP - Colorado Self-Exclusion Assistance
administering_body: idPair / NVSEP (assists with Colorado Division of Gaming program)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org
duration_options: One year, three years, or five years (same as state program)
notes: Colorado is a founding NVSEP state (launched August 2024). NVSEP assists consumers with enrollment in the Colorado Division of Gaming self-exclusion program covering casinos and sports betting. idPair operates the program.

program_name: Colorado DFS / PrizePicks-Underdog-Dabble-Splash Sports - 50-State Self-Exclusion (idPair)
administering_body: idPair / Coalition for Fantasy Sports (PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports)
enrollment_method: Online via operator apps / idPair platform
notes: March 2026: PrizePicks, Underdog, Dabble, and Splash Sports (via Coalition for Fantasy Sports) launched first 50-state self-exclusion program with idPair covering DFS/pick'em across all 50 states simultaneously. Colorado DFS self-exclusion previously operator-level. This covers Colorado.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program — Colorado Lottery (confirmed)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Colorado Lottery official responsible gaming pages (Know Your Limits; Protect Yourself; FAQ) reviewed. The Colorado Lottery itself has no account-level self-exclusion program. Third-party courier apps operating in Colorado (Jackpocket; Jackpot.com) offer their own account self-exclusion (Jackpocket: 30/60/90-day or permanent; Jackpot.com: 3 months to lifetime) but these are operator-level tools not administered by the Colorado Lottery. The Lottery provides BetBlocker referral and 1-800-GAMBLER helpline only.

program_name: Connecticut Gaming Self-Exclusion Program (DCP Statewide)
administering_body: Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection (DCP), Gaming Division
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (tribal: Foxwoods; Mohegan Sun);iGaming/online casino;Sports betting (retail and online);DFS/fantasy sports;Lottery (iLottery/online keno);Charitable gaming
enrollment_method: Online portal at gaming-exclusion.service.ct.gov OR in-person at Foxwoods or Mohegan Sun
duration_options: One year, five years, or lifetime
removal_process: Written removal request to casino Director of Security (tribal properties); for DCP portal exclusions: request after minimum period expires; lifetime is irrevocable
notes: Statewide program launched 2021 under Public Act 21-23. Covers online casino, sports wagering, fantasy contests, keno/lottery (iLottery), and commercial/tribal gaming. Foxwoods and Mohegan Sun are tribal but participate in DCP statewide list; self-exclusion can be submitted in-person at either tribal casino. DCP email: dcp.selfexclusion@ct.gov. OTB/parimutuel horse racing under Sportech (Winners OTB) may be covered by the broader DCP mandate but specific confirmation not found - see notes. No NVSEP coverage confirmed for CT as of May 2026.

program_name: Delaware Lottery Self-Exclusion Program
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (video lottery agents);iGaming/online casino;Sports betting (sports lottery)
enrollment_method: In person at Delaware Lottery office (appointment recommended); must present valid government-issued photo ID
removal_process: After minimum period expires, appear in person at Lottery office to request reinstatement; lifetime exclusion cannot be removed
notes: Program covers all licensed video lottery agents (Delaware Park Casino, Bally's Dover Resort & Casino, Harrington Raceway & Casino) and their iGaming websites and the Lottery's iGaming/Sports Mobile app. Violation is a Class A misdemeanor; winnings forfeited. Two separate pages on delottery.com (Table Games and iGaming) describe the same statewide program.

program_name: Delaware Fantasy Sports Self-Exclusion (Operator-level)
enrollment_method: Operator account-based (online); reported via Delaware DFS reports as player-initiated self-exclusion
notes: Delaware DFS regulations require operators to establish self-exclusion procedures. Annual reports track counts (e.g., 62 self-exclusions in 2020 reporting period). Program is operator-administered under DGE oversight. No separate statewide DFS self-exclusion portal found.

program_name: Delaware Lottery Self-Exclusion Program (covers video lottery at racetracks; parimutuel not explicitly included)
verticals_covered: video lottery at licensed agents (Delaware Park Casino; Harrington Raceway & Casino; Bally's Dover);iGaming websites;Lottery mobile app — parimutuel wagering not explicitly covered
enrollment_method: In-person appointment at Delaware Lottery office (1575 McKee Road Suite 102; Dover DE 19904); call (302) 744-1669
removal_process: In-person at Lottery office after exclusion period expires; lifetime is permanent and irrevocable
notes: Delaware Lottery self-exclusion covers 'all gaming activities at licensed Delaware video lottery agents' including Delaware Park Casino and Harrington Raceway & Casino plus their iGaming sites. The program is explicitly tied to video lottery operations. The Delaware Thoroughbred and Harness Racing Commissions have no separate self-exclusion program for parimutuel wagering. Parimutuel wagering is not mentioned in the Lottery self-exclusion documentation. Classified as state-run for video lottery vertical; parimutuel wagering at same racetracks has no confirmed separate exclusion mechanism.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — Delaware Board of Charitable Gaming)
administering_body: Delaware Board of Charitable Gaming (Division of Professional Regulation)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Delaware Board of Charitable Gaming official website and FAQs reviewed. No self-exclusion program exists. Sponsoring organizations are only required to post the DCGP helpline (1-888-850-8888). No self-exclusion provisions appear in the board's regulations (10 Del. Admin. Code 100) or enabling statute (28 Del. C. ch. 15).

program_name: Florida Gaming Control Commission Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program (Pari-Mutuel Wagering, Cardrooms, Slots)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (slot facilities in Miami-Dade/Broward);Horse/parimutuel racing;Charitable gaming (cardrooms)
enrollment_method: In person at any Florida FGCC/Division of Pari-Mutuel Wagering facility; must present valid photo ID; photograph and physical description taken
duration_options: 1 year to lifetime (minimum 6 months noted in FCCG materials; FGCC materials say minimum 1 year)
removal_process: Not confirmed; person may request removal after minimum period per general FGCC guidance
notes: FGCC Exclusion List page at flgaming.gov/pmw/exclusion. Covers pari-mutuel wagering sites, cardrooms, and licensed slot facilities. Florida Lottery does NOT appear to have its own self-exclusion program — Florida Lottery responsible gaming page references problem gambling helpline only. Seminole tribal casino self-exclusion is separate (property/tribal level).

program_name: Seminole Tribe / Hard Rock Bet Self-Exclusion
administering_body: Seminole Tribe of Florida / Hard Rock Digital
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (Seminole tribal);Sports betting (Hard Rock Bet online)
enrollment_method: In app via Hard Rock Bet (Responsible Gaming > Self-Exclusion) or at Seminole tribal property
duration_options: 6 months; 1 year; permanent (irreversible during period for app; property exclusion options may differ)
removal_process: Self-exclusion during term is permanent/irreversible per Hard Rock Bet app
notes: Seminole-Florida compact requires tribal operation to maintain voluntary self-exclusion. Hard Rock Bet responsible gaming site is rg.hardrock.bet. Tribal casino self-exclusion is property-level / tribal. Not administered by Florida state gaming agency.

program_name: No self-exclusion program for Florida Lottery
enrollment_method: none
notes: Official FL Lottery responsible-gaming page lists helpline referrals (888-ADMIT-IT; 1-800-MY-RESET; Gamblers Anonymous) and budget tips but no self-exclusion mechanism. FL Lottery is retail-only; no iLottery online sales. Self-exclusion is absent from all enumerated RG tools.

program_name: Georgia Lottery Corporation Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program
verticals_covered: Lottery (online/mobile lottery play — iHOPE account; Diggi Games; online ticket purchase)
enrollment_method: Online via Georgia Lottery account / call Player Information Hotline at 1-800-GA-LUCKY (1-800-425-8259)
duration_options: 3 months; 6 months; 1 year; 2 years; 5 years; 10 years (irrevocable once selected)
removal_process: Not permitted during selected period; must call Player Information Hotline to request reactivation after period expires
notes: Program covers online/mobile lottery play (iHOPE account), including Diggi Games. Does NOT appear to cover retail/in-store ticket purchase (no mechanism to enforce at point of sale). Georgia earned NCPG iCAP accreditation for online responsible gambling. No evidence of DFS-specific state self-exclusion program; Georgia has no authorizing DFS statute — operator-level only via CFS/NVSEP.

program_name: Georgia DFS — operator-level / national NVSEP (CFS operators)
enrollment_method: Online via individual operator apps (PrizePicks, Underdog, Dabble, Splash Sports)
notes: Georgia has no DFS authorizing statute; operators run under general rules. Four leading DFS operators (PrizePicks, Underdog, Dabble, Splash Sports) launched an all-50-state self-exclusion program via idPair/CFS effective March 2026. Covers Georgia by default. No state-run program.

program_name: No gambling — no self-exclusion program
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: All forms of gambling prohibited under Hawaii Revised Statutes Chapter 712. No authorized verticals; no self-exclusion program exists or is needed.

program_name: No self-exclusion program for Idaho Lottery
enrollment_method: none
notes: Play Wise / responsible gaming pages list age requirement and NCPG helpline only; no self-exclusion or account-restriction tool. Idaho Lottery has no iLottery online sales channel. Self-exclusion is not enumerated among RG tools.

program_name: No state self-exclusion for Idaho horse/parimutuel racing
enrollment_method: none
notes: Idaho Racing Commission RG page references NCPG helpline only; no voluntary self-exclusion rule found in IDAPA 11. HHR banned since 2015. ADW is licensed and active; individual ADW operators (TVG/FanDuel Racing etc.) may offer account-level self-exclusion per NTRA best practices but no IRB-mandated central program exists.

program_name: No self-exclusion for Idaho charitable gaming (bingo/raffles)
enrollment_method: none
notes: Idaho Lottery regulates bingo and raffles only (poker runs/casino nights prohibited). Charitable gaming FAQ and rules pages contain no responsible-gaming or self-exclusion content. No self-exclusion program of any kind applies to charitable gaming in Idaho.

program_name: Illinois Gaming Board Self-Exclusion Program (SEP)
enrollment_method: In person at any IGB office located at Illinois casinos; no appointment required; takes approx. 30-45 minutes
duration_options: Lifetime (only option per IGB; after 5 years may request removal)
removal_process: After minimum 5 years, must submit affidavit/letter from licensed mental health professional / certified gambling addiction counselor or psychiatrist attesting person no longer is a problem gambler
notes: IGB SEP established 2002; expanded to include sports wagering June 2019. As of March 2025, 15,613 individuals on SEP list. Video gaming terminals (VGTs) NOT currently in scope; IGB solicited public comment Jan 2025 on proposed expansion to VGTs; rule not yet adopted as of research date. Exclusion also extends to Caesar's and Boyd Gaming properties nationwide.

program_name: Illinois Lottery Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program
enrollment_method: Mail or in-person delivery of signed and notarized Voluntary Self-Exclusion Agreement to Illinois Lottery Claims Unit; form available at any Lottery office or downloaded from illinoislottery.com; also by calling 1-800-252-1775
duration_options: Minimum 5 years (exclusion from online play); program covers both online and in-store
removal_process: No reinstatement process described; minimum 5-year term
notes: Illinois Lottery VSE Agreement covers both online and in-store lottery play. Self-excluded persons are removed from marketing lists and denied prize payments over $600 at lottery payment centers. Governed by Ill. Admin. Code tit. 11 § 1770.240. Signed agreement must be notarized.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion for Illinois horse/parimutuel racing (IRB)
enrollment_method: none
notes: 11 Ill. Adm. Code 1322.80 is involuntary track-initiated exclusion (notification to IRB and USTA); it is NOT a voluntary self-exclusion program. IGB SEP covers casinos and sports wagering only — horse racing and ADW are explicitly outside IGB jurisdiction. IRB website lists no voluntary SE program. ADW operators may offer individual account-level limits but no IRB-mandated central program.

program_name: idPair/Coalition for Fantasy Sports DFS Self-Exclusion (all-operator; all-50-states)
enrollment_method: online via participating operator platforms
duration_options: not specified — permanent excluded until removal requested
notes: CFS/idPair expanded DFS self-exclusion to all 50 states March 2026. Illinois was included in earlier Aug-2025 expansion to 15 states. Operators: PrizePicks; Underdog; Betr; Dabble; Splash Sports. No IL state-run DFS program exists (IL DFS is legal but unregulated per 2020 IL Supreme Court; IGB issued C&Ds to pick-em operators 2025). idpair_flag=yes; is_nvsep=no (this is CFS/idPair program not NVSEP). Enrollment through individual operator platforms.

program_name: Indiana Gaming Commission Voluntary Exclusion Program (VEP)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;Horse/parimutuel racing (off-track betting facilities);Sports betting (retail sportsbooks)
enrollment_method: In person at any Indiana casino or IGC Indianapolis office; with a designated problem gambling treatment provider; or online via IGC website with identity verification
removal_process: Upon expiration of selected term, request removal via form at IGC office, Indiana casino location, by US mail, or online on IGC website
notes: VEP effective July 1, 2004. Covers all Indiana casinos and off-track betting facilities. Sports wagering certificates added per Sports Wagering Act. Note: VEP covers retail sports wagering; online sports wagering has a separate Internet Self-Restriction Program (ISRP). NVSEP page exists at nvsep.org/indiana — Indiana participates in NVSEP/idPair program.

program_name: Indiana Gaming Commission Internet Self-Restriction Program (ISRP)
enrollment_method: Online via IGC website at in.gov/igc with identity verification
removal_process: Upon expiration of term, request removal at IGC office, Indiana casino, by US mail, or online on IGC website
notes: ISRP created under Indiana Code 4-38; applies ONLY to mobile/online sports wagering. Does not cover land-based casino. Operators required to stop all direct marketing to ISRP participants and cancel wagers. Separate from the VEP (which covers retail sports and casino).

program_name: Indiana — NVSEP / idPair National Program
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org; assists with Indiana IGC VEP enrollment process
duration_options: Mirrors IGC VEP options (1 year; 5 years; lifetime)
notes: NVSEP has a dedicated Indiana page (nvsep.org/indiana). Provides free digital assistance with Indiana IGC VEP enrollment. Verified as a participating NVSEP jurisdiction.

program_name: No self-exclusion program for Hoosier Lottery
administering_body: Hoosier Lottery / Indiana State Lottery Commission
enrollment_method: none
notes: Keep Play Positive page lists helpline (1-800-994-8448); Track Your Play calculator; Positive Play Quiz; educational videos — but no self-exclusion or account-restriction mechanism. No iLottery in Indiana (HB 1078 failed). WLA Level 4 status but SE not offered.

program_name: No self-exclusion for Indiana charitable gaming (bingo/raffles/pull tabs)
administering_body: Indiana Gaming Commission — Charity Gaming Division
enrollment_method: none
notes: IGC Voluntary Exclusion Program covers Indiana casinos only. Charitable gaming (bingo/raffles/pull tabs) is regulated under IC 4-32.3 separately; IGC charity-gaming page and VEP documentation confirm VEP does not extend to charitable gaming venues. No self-exclusion program exists for charitable gaming.

program_name: Indiana DFS — operator-level / national NVSEP (CFS operators)
enrollment_method: Online via individual DFS operator apps (PrizePicks, Underdog, Dabble, Splash Sports)
notes: Indiana IGC regulates DFS (Paid Fantasy Sports Division). No state-run DFS self-exclusion program identified. CFS DFS operators (PrizePicks, Underdog, Dabble, Splash Sports) operate all-50-state self-exclusion via idPair/NVSEP effective March 2026.

program_name: Iowa Statewide Self-Exclusion Program (IRGC)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;Horse/parimutuel racing;Sports betting (retail and online);DFS
enrollment_method: In person at IRGC office (by appointment); in person at any licensed casino or racetrack; in person at Iowa Gambling Treatment Provider agency; by mail (notarized form)
duration_options: Five years (auto-expires) or lifetime (irrevocable for enrollments on/after 07/01/2017)
removal_process: Five-year term auto-expires; lifetime enrollees who enrolled before 07/01/2017 may submit written revocation request to IRGC (up to 10 business days processing); post-2017 lifetime enrollments cannot be revoked
notes: Covers all casino, racetrack, sports wagering, and fantasy sports operators licensed by IRGC per Iowa Code 99D.7(23) and 99F.4(22). Lottery NOT covered — separate Iowa Lottery program required. Charitable gaming (bingo/raffles under DIAL) not covered. NVSEP assists with notarization but Iowa program remains state-run.

program_name: Iowa Lottery Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program
enrollment_method: In person at any Iowa Lottery regional office (notarization required; notaries available on-site)
duration_options: Five years (renewable) or lifetime (irrevocable for agreements signed on/after 07/01/2017)
removal_process: Pre-07/01/2017 lifetime enrollees may submit removal request form to regional office after five years on list; post-2017 lifetime enrollments irrevocable; five-year terms renewable
notes: Separate and distinct from IRGC program. Enrollment bans prize claims over $600 at lottery regional offices and removes enrollee from marketing. Enrollees are not automatically placed on IRGC list; must enroll separately in both programs to cover both lottery and casino/sports verticals.

program_name: NVSEP — Iowa (idPair self-exclusion assistance)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;Horse/parimutuel racing;Sports betting (retail and online);DFS
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org (free remote online notarization provided via approved notary vendor)
duration_options: Matches duration options of the underlying IRGC state program (five years or lifetime)
removal_process: Follows underlying IRGC revocation process; NVSEP facilitates access but does not replace state program
notes: Iowa was one of four launch states for NVSEP in August 2024 (with CO, MI, TN). idPair provides free remote online notarization to facilitate enrollment in the IRGC statewide program; no changes to Iowa's underlying program terms. NVSEP does not replace the state program; covers same IRGC-regulated verticals (casino/racetrack/sports/DFS). Iowa Lottery vertical not covered by NVSEP.

program_name: NVSEP DFS All-50-States Program (idPair / PrizePicks / Underdog / Dabble / Splash Sports)
administering_body: idPair (with PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports — Coalition for Fantasy Sports)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org or participating operator apps (single enrollment excludes from all four DFS operators simultaneously)
duration_options: Matches options provided by participating DFS operators
notes: Launched March 10, 2026 covering all 50 states including Iowa. Operator-led program via Coalition for Fantasy Sports (CFS). Single enrollment excludes user from PrizePicks, Underdog, Dabble, and Splash Sports. Separate from IRGC's existing DFS coverage. Can expand to additional verticals.

program_name: No Program — Charitable Gaming
administering_body: Iowa Department of Inspections; Appeals & Licensing (DIAL)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Charitable gaming in Iowa (bingo, raffles, game nights) licensed by DIAL, not IRGC. No statewide self-exclusion program identified for charitable gaming. IRGC self-exclusion does not cover charitable gaming venues.

program_name: Kansas Voluntary Exclusion Program (VEP) — Casinos
enrollment_method: In person at KRGC office (Topeka) or at any of the four state-owned Kansas casinos (Boot Hill; Kansas Star; Hollywood Casino at Kansas Speedway; Kansas Crossing)
removal_process: Two-year: after expiration applicant must (1) complete a problem gambling assessment from a Kansas Certified Gambling Counselor and (2) complete healthy lifestyle courses; KRGC makes final determination on removal. Lifetime: no removal.
notes: Covers the four state-owned destination casinos operated by management contractors under Kansas Lottery oversight: Boot Hill (Dodge City), Kansas Star (Mulvane), Hollywood Casino at Kansas Speedway (Kansas City), Kansas Crossing (Pittsburg). No tribal casinos in Kansas.

program_name: Kansas Sports Wagering Exclusion Program
enrollment_method: Secure document upload via KRGC secure file transfer portal (email to Garrett Rogers for credentials; applicant uploads completed form and government-issued photo ID)
removal_process: Enrollees must complete exclusion period; no premature removal per program rules
notes: Separate program from the casino VEP. Disables existing sports wagering accounts and prevents creation of new accounts. Specific duration options not confirmed on official page — needs human review to verify full term options.

program_name: Kansas Lottery iLottery Responsible Gaming / Self-Exclusion
enrollment_method: Online via Kansas Lottery iLottery account (playonkansas.com)
duration_options: Temporary (account-level break) or permanent account closure
removal_process: Permanent closure is irreversible; temporary breaks expire per selected term; account-based only (does not affect retail ticket purchases)
notes: Kansas Lottery offers account-level self-exclusion for its iLottery platform; prevents login and online play/promotions. Does NOT prevent purchase of retail lottery tickets at physical stores. No statewide retail lottery self-exclusion program identified. Horse/parimutuel racing self-exclusion under KRGC not separately confirmed; may be covered under VEP — needs human review.

program_name: No Program — Charitable Gaming
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Charitable bingo in Kansas licensed by Kansas Department of Revenue. No self-exclusion program identified for charitable gaming. Horse/parimutuel racing self-exclusion — no separate confirmed program found; KRGC VEP focused on state-owned casinos.

program_name: Kentucky Horse Racing and Gaming Corporation (KHRC) — Statewide Self-Exclusion List (Horse Racing / HHR)
administering_body: Kentucky Horse Racing and Gaming Corporation (KHRC/KHRGC)
enrollment_method: At the track (in person); online via operator website; by phone through licensed racing association
duration_options: Temporary or permanent (specific term lengths not specified in regulation)
removal_process: Request through licensed racing association; KHRC compiles comprehensive list monthly and distributes to all racing associations
notes: Per 810 KAR 2:100, KHRC maintains a comprehensive statewide self-exclusion list compiled monthly from all licensed racing associations and distributed back to all licensees. Covers gambling at racing tracks and HHR. Separate from sports wagering regulation under 809 KAR.

program_name: Kentucky Horse Racing and Gaming Corporation (KHRC) — Statewide Self-Exclusion List (Sports Wagering)
administering_body: Kentucky Horse Racing and Gaming Corporation (KHRC/KHRGC)
enrollment_method: At licensed retail sportsbook premises; online via licensee-specific website or mobile app; by phone
removal_process: Each operator submits new exclusions monthly to KHRC via approved portal; KHRC compiles comprehensive list and redistributes to all licensees at least monthly
notes: Per 809 KAR 10:007, KHRC compiles and maintains comprehensive statewide list of voluntarily self-excluded sports wagering patrons. Operator-collected information flows up to KHRC and is redistributed monthly to all licensees. Confidential; exempt from Kentucky Open Records Act. DFS (fantasy contests) not yet formally regulated under a separate KHRC self-exclusion program as of research date (HB 904 2026 overhaul pending/vetoed).

program_name: Kentucky Lottery — Online Self-Exclusion (Fun Club / iLottery)
enrollment_method: Online via play.kylottery.com account settings
duration_options: 6 months; 1 year; 2 years; 5 years; or permanent closure
removal_process: Temporary terms auto-expire and account reopens; permanent closure is irreversible; cannot create second account after permanent self-exclusion
notes: Account-level self-exclusion for Kentucky Lottery online/iLottery platform. Does not cover retail lottery ticket purchases. Does not automatically exclude from KHRC-regulated verticals (sports betting, horse racing).

program_name: No self-exclusion for Kentucky charitable gaming (bingo/raffles)
administering_body: Kentucky Horse Racing & Gaming / Office of Charitable Gaming
enrollment_method: none
notes: KY Office of Charitable Gaming (now under KHRG post-HB 904) website contains no reference to self-exclusion or responsible-gaming tools for bingo/raffles. Charitable gaming SE is not enumerated among any KY regulatory program. KY sports-betting and horse-racing SE programs (KHRG) do not extend to charitable gaming.

program_name: Louisiana Gaming Control Board — Voluntary Exclusion Program (Casino)
administering_body: Louisiana Gaming Control Board (LGCB) / Louisiana State Police Gaming Enforcement Division
enrollment_method: In person at Louisiana State Police Gaming Enforcement Division location (7901 Independence Blvd; Baton Rouge); by mail (with advance LGCB approval); online enrollment also available per secondary sources
removal_process: After expiration of five years from date of exclusion; submit written request to LGCB Administrative Hearing Office; Hearing Officer determines whether basis for continued exclusion remains; removal requires recommendation from qualified mental health professional
notes: LGCB's voluntary exclusion list covers casino gaming at all LGCB-regulated establishments and sports wagering platforms. Single enrollment effectively covers all casinos and licensed sports wagering operators. In-person enrollment required at Louisiana State Police Gaming Enforcement Division; 1-888-295-8450. Louisiana Lottery has no separate iLottery platform; no lottery self-exclusion program identified.

program_name: No Program — Lottery
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Louisiana Lottery does not sell tickets online (no iLottery); all sales are in-person at retail. No Louisiana Lottery self-exclusion program identified.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program for horse racing/ADW (LGCB exclusion covers casino only; Racing Commission has none)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: The LGCB voluntary exclusion program covers casino gaming at all LGCB-regulated establishments (riverboat casinos; pari-mutuel live racing facility casinos; land-based casino). The language targets casino gaming activities only. The Louisiana State Racing Commission — which regulates horse racing and parimutuel wagering — has no separate voluntary self-exclusion program for racing/ADW. No evidence of a central state exclusion list for horse racing wagering. ADW operators may maintain operator-level tools but no state-administered program found. Retrieval based on LGCB voluntary exclusions page and LSP Gaming Enforcement problem gambling page.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — charitable gaming not regulated by LGCB)
administering_body: Louisiana Department of Revenue — Office of Charitable Gaming
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: The LGCB FAQ explicitly states: 'The Louisiana Gaming Control Board does not regulate fundraising, raffles, or bingo.' Charitable gaming is regulated by the Louisiana Department of Revenue, Office of Charitable Gaming (OCG). The OCG website contains no self-exclusion or responsible gaming program. Louisiana charitable gaming statutes (La. Rev. Stat. Tit. 4; La. Admin. Code Tit. 42) contain no self-exclusion provisions. No self-exclusion program exists for charitable gaming in Louisiana.

program_name: Maine Gambling Control Unit — Self-Exclusion Program (Casino)
administering_body: Maine Gambling Control Unit (Gambling Control Board)
enrollment_method: Online (virtual via Zoom with ADEPT Problem Gambling Services Coordinator); in person at licensed casino; in person at designated Office of Substance Abuse sites
duration_options: 1 year; 3 years; 5 years; or lifetime (lifetime requires completing a shorter term first)
removal_process: Request to Gambling Control Unit; specific removal process details not confirmed on official page; needs human review
notes: Maine Gambling Control Unit holds official list; distributes to licensed casinos. Virtual option launched March 2023 via ADEPT/AdCare. Lottery not covered. No specific horse/parimutuel racing (harness) self-exclusion program identified under Harness Racing Commission — GCU regulates advance deposit wagering but no separate harness-specific list found.

program_name: Maine Gambling Control Unit — Self-Exclusion Program (Sports Wagering)
administering_body: Maine Gambling Control Unit (Gambling Control Board)
enrollment_method: Online (virtual via Zoom with ADEPT Coordinator; can also self-exclude via operator platform/app); in person at licensed casino or GCB office
duration_options: 1 year; 3 years; 5 years; or lifetime (lifetime requires completing a shorter term first)
notes: Separate exclusion list maintained for sports wagering operators. GCU holds official list and distributes only to sports wagering operators. Operators can also accept self-exclusion through their own platform per Maine GCB Chapter 13 rules adopted October 2025. As of 2025 data: 30 new sports betting self-exclusions, 87.5% increase year-over-year.

program_name: No Program — Lottery
administering_body: Maine State Lottery (Bureau of Alcoholic Beverages and Lottery Operations)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Maine State Lottery does not operate iLottery; retail only. No Maine Lottery self-exclusion program identified. Maine GCU self-exclusion covers casinos and sports betting but not lottery.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program for horse racing/ADW (Maine GCU SE covers casino and sports wagering only)
administering_body: Maine Gambling Control Unit (Department of Public Safety)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Maine GCU operates self-exclusion lists for (1) casino/slot facilities and (2) sports wagering. ADW/advance deposit wagering and horse racing parimutuel wagering are not listed as covered verticals by the GCU self-exclusion program. The ADEPT Maine self-exclusion locations page confirms coverage for casinos and sports wagering only; ADW/horse racing is not mentioned. Harness racing is regulated by the Maine State Harness Racing Commission (DACF); ADW is licensed by the GCU. Neither body administers a voluntary self-exclusion list for racing/ADW. ADW license-holder (Penn ADW/Hollywoodraces.com) may offer operator-level tools but no state-administered program confirmed.

program_name: Maryland Lottery and Gaming Control Agency — Voluntary Exclusion Program (VEP)
administering_body: Maryland Lottery and Gaming Control Agency (MLGCA)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;Lottery;Sports betting (retail and online);DFS;Electronic instant bingo
enrollment_method: In person at MLGCA headquarters (Baltimore) or at any of Maryland's licensed casinos; in extenuating circumstances may mail notarized application with prior approval of Responsible Gaming Program Director
removal_process: Two-year: after minimum two years submit formal application with documentation — one of: problem gambling assessment from licensed professional; completion of approved treatment program; or approved healthy decision-making program; MLGCA makes final determination. Lifetime: no removal. Removal is NOT automatic.
notes: Single unified VEP covering casinos, Maryland Lottery games, sports wagering, daily fantasy sports, and electronic instant bingo halls. Telephone: 410-230-8798; email: responsible.gaming@maryland.gov. Program established 2011; expanded to sports wagering and DFS. Maryland Racing Commission (horse racing under Dept. of Labor) is separate — no MLGCA VEP coverage for horse racing; see notes.

program_name: No state voluntary exclusion program for horse racing/ADW (MLGCA VEP covers lottery/casino/sports/DFS/bingo; Racing Commission has no SE program)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Maryland's Voluntary Exclusion Program (MLGCA/mdgaming.com) covers Maryland Lottery; Maryland casinos; electronic instant bingo halls; sports wagering; daily fantasy sports. Horse racing and ADW parimutuel wagering are not included. The Maryland Racing Commission (COMAR Title 09 Subtitle 10) has six chapters covering thoroughbred rules; harness racing; prohibited acts; general; satellite simulcast betting; new racetrack license — none include voluntary self-exclusion provisions. ADW operators (TwinSpires; TVG/FanDuel Racing) maintain operator-level account self-exclusion independently. No state-administered program for horse racing/ADW confirmed.

program_name: Massachusetts Gaming Commission — Voluntary Self-Exclusion (VSE) Program
enrollment_method: Phone: 1-800-GAM-1234 (24-hour); online via gamesensema.com / PlayWell; in person at PlayWell (GameSense) Info Centers at Plainridge Park Casino; MGM Springfield; Encore Boston Harbor
duration_options: One year; three years; five years; or lifetime (lifetime available only after completing a shorter term)
removal_process: Must complete a reinstatement session with trained professional who discusses safe gaming tips; risks; and referrals to additional help before removal
notes: Covers casino gaming floors AND retail/digital sports wagering. Dual self-exclusion option available for both verticals simultaneously. Casino VSE launched 2015; sports wagering VSE launched January 31, 2023. As of March 2023 (March Madness) both lists fully operational. Lottery NOT covered — MGC VSE does not apply to Massachusetts State Lottery. Contact: 617-533-9737 or vse@massgaming.gov.

program_name: No Program — Lottery
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Massachusetts Lottery is not covered by the MGC VSE program. No separate Massachusetts Lottery self-exclusion program identified. Massachusetts Lottery looking at potential iLottery future launch but no online platform or self-exclusion confirmed as of research date.

program_name: Massachusetts Gaming Commission Voluntary Self-Exclusion — Casino and Parimutuel/Simulcast Areas
verticals_covered: commercial casino;parimutuel horse racing;simulcast wagering;sports betting
enrollment_method: Online (PlayWell 24/7 chat); phone (1-800-426-1234 24-hour line); in-person at PlayWell Info Centers at casinos or MGC Boston office
removal_process: Enrollment in a shorter period required before lifetime; duration cannot be shortened after enrollment; no public removal process stated for fixed terms
notes: 205 CMR 133.00 explicitly covers 'entering the gaming area of a gaming establishment or any area in which pari-mutuel or simulcasting wagers are placed.' This language encompasses licensed horse racing venues and simulcast facilities regulated by the MGC Division of Racing. The MGC administers a single statewide VSE program covering casino gaming; sports wagering; and parimutuel/simulcast wagering areas. Enrollment is through the PlayWell program. The VSE FAQ (March 2023) confirms four duration options. Per the massgaming.com horse racing page; the general VSE program applies to all MGC-regulated facilities including racing.

program_name: Massachusetts NVSEP participation — CONFIRMED NEXT; not yet live as of 2026-05-23
enrollment_method: Not yet live (pending MGC activation; enrollment expected at nvsep.org once live)
notes: As of December 2025 (most recent confirmed data point) Wyoming became the 21st NVSEP jurisdiction and Massachusetts was confirmed 'next' but not yet live. No evidence found of a Massachusetts NVSEP activation announcement in early 2026. MACGH advocacy page links to nvsep.org in footer confirming organizational involvement. The MGC's own VSE (205 CMR 133) is already live and covers casino + parimutuel + sports (see id=72); this NVSEP row specifically tracks the national multi-state program. idpair_flag=yes because idPair/NVSEP is the program vehicle. Status should be re-checked; may have launched after May 2026 data cutoff.

program_name: NVSEP DFS All-50-States Program (idPair / PrizePicks / Underdog / Dabble / Splash Sports)
administering_body: idPair (with PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports — Coalition for Fantasy Sports)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org or participating operator apps
notes: Launched March 10, 2026 covering all 50 states including Massachusetts. Operator-led program via Coalition for Fantasy Sports (CFS) with PrizePicks, Underdog, Dabble, Splash Sports. Single enrollment simultaneously excludes from all four DFS operators. Massachusetts DFS separately regulated by MGC under 940 CMR 34.00.

program_name: Property-by-property tribal casino self-exclusion (no central MN state list)
administering_body: Individual tribal gaming operations (11 Ojibwe and Dakota nations; 19 casino properties)
enrollment_method: Varies by property — in-person at individual tribal casino
duration_options: Varies by property — not uniformly specified
removal_process: Varies by property — not uniformly specified
notes: Minnesota has 19 tribal casino properties operated by 11 tribal nations; all gaming is conducted under tribal-state compacts. There is no state-administered central voluntary self-exclusion list. Minnesota tribal compacts do not uniformly mandate self-exclusion programs. Individual tribes such as the Shakopee Mdewakanton Sioux Community (Mystic Lake; Little Six) and Mille Lacs Band of Ojibwe offer property-level self-exclusion. AGA 2025 Regulatory Fact Sheet confirms no statewide centralized list. Each patron must contact individual properties to self-exclude. MIGA FAQ does not mention a central program.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — Minnesota Lottery)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: The Minnesota Lottery gambling-help page and FAQ contain no self-exclusion program. Responsible gaming resources are limited to the problem gambling helpline (1-800-333-HOPE) and links to external treatment providers. The lottery's account registration and FAQ pages make no mention of self-exclusion or account-level deposit/spend limits. A Kindbridge 2025-26 state-by-state review confirms Minnesota does not maintain a statewide centralized exclusion list for lottery. No self-exclusion tool found on mnlottery.com.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — Minnesota charitable gaming)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: The Minnesota Gambling Control Board regulates lawful charitable gambling (pull-tabs; bingo; paddlewheels; tipboards; raffles) under Minnesota Statutes Chapter 349. The GCB website and Charitable Gambling Manual contain no self-exclusion provisions. The Board's only problem gambling reference is the helpline (800-333-HOPE). Charitable gaming in Minnesota is conducted by licensed nonprofit organizations at permitted premises; no central patron exclusion list exists or is required under state law.

program_name: No state voluntary self-exclusion program for horse racing/ADW (Racing Commission has involuntary exclusion only; no SE for ADW)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Minnesota Statutes 240.27 grants the Racing Commission authority to exclude individuals with felony convictions or license violations (involuntary exclusion only) and allows racetracks to eject patrons for lawful reasons. There is no voluntary self-exclusion program in Chapter 240 or MRC rules. The MRC website links only to the Minnesota Alliance for Problem Gambling for responsible gaming; no SE program page exists. The ADW operator licensing page (mn.gov) imposes no SE requirements on licensed ADW providers. Individual racetracks (Canterbury Park; Running Aces) may have property-level patron self-restriction tools but no central state program found.

program_name: Mississippi Gaming Commission Self-Exclusion Program
enrollment_method: Online enrollment only — email address required; upload photo ID and selfie photos; can use casino-provided device if no personal access
duration_options: Minimum 3 years; longer periods available; no lifetime option until at least 3 years served
removal_process: Automatic deactivation at end of term — no action needed; casinos may take up to 10 business days to update system
notes: Program is statewide for all MGC-licensed casinos. Per FAQ: does NOT cover MS Lottery or bingo. Sports betting in MS is on-premises/casino-property only so casino exclusion effectively covers retail sports betting. Contact: selfexclusion@mgc.ms.gov or 228-297-8174.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — Mississippi Lottery)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Mississippi Lottery Corporation offers no self-exclusion program. The Play Responsibly page (mslottery.com/players/play-responsibly/) and FAQs reference only the 1-800-GAMBLER helpline. The Mississippi Gaming Commission self-exclusion program explicitly excludes lottery play — MGC FAQ states: 'Enrollment in the MGC self-exclusion program does not prohibit you from playing the MS lottery or bingo.' No lottery-specific SE program exists at mslottery.com.

program_name: Missouri Gaming Commission List of Disassociated Persons (DAP)
enrollment_method: Online via MGC portal; in person at any licensed Missouri casino or Jefferson City MGC office
duration_options: Lifetime enrollment; petition for removal available after 5 years minimum; re-enrollment after removal is permanent for life
removal_process: Written petition to MGC after minimum 5 years; photo ID and photo required; re-enrollment makes exclusion irrevocable for life
notes: Established 1996 as List of Disassociated Persons. Covers all licensed Missouri excursion gambling boats. Does not extend to out-of-state or tribal casinos. Per regulation, persons on DAP list are also prohibited from sports wagering.

program_name: Missouri Gaming Commission List of Self-Excluded Persons (SEP) — Sports Wagering
verticals_covered: Sports betting (online and retail; launched December 1 2025)
enrollment_method: Online portal or in person at MGC office; application requires photo ID; cannot enroll another person
duration_options: 5-year enrollment; auto-removed at expiration; renewal option available during 3 months before expiration
removal_process: Automatic removal at end of 5 years; renewal option for additional 5-year period(s) available 3 months prior to expiration
notes: Separate list from DAP (casino exclusion). Specific to sports wagering. Operators must remove SEP individuals from marketing lists and deny participation in player programs. Sports wagering launched December 1 2025.

program_name: Missouri Lottery Self-Exclusion Program (SEP)
verticals_covered: Lottery (prize claims; player loyalty program; marketing)
enrollment_method: Paper form only — must be notarized; mail or hand-deliver to MO Lottery offices in Jefferson City, Kansas City, Springfield, or St. Louis
removal_process: Not described; lifetime agreement; contact MO Lottery Responsible Gaming Coordinator
notes: Program established August 1 2013. Prohibits prize claims over $600; access to My Lottery Players Club; receipt of email/text alerts. Separate from MGC casino/sports wagering programs.

program_name: Sports Bet Montana In-App Self-Exclusion (Montana Lottery)
verticals_covered: Sports betting (Montana Lottery kiosks and Sports Bet Montana app)
enrollment_method: In-app via account settings in the Sports Bet Montana app
duration_options: Unspecified — in-app self-exclusion available; deposit/loss limits and cool-off periods also available
notes: Sports Bet Montana (operated by Montana Lottery) provides in-app responsible gaming tools including full self-exclusion via account settings. Duration options not publicly specified on official site.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — property-by-property only)
administering_body: Montana Department of Justice Gambling Control Division (no statewide SE list)
enrollment_method: N/A — no statewide program; individual operators only
notes: Montana has no statewide centralized self-exclusion list for gambling. The DOJ Gambling Control Division compulsive-gambling page lists helpline numbers only (Montana Council on Problem Gambling: 888-552-9076) with no self-exclusion enrollment. MCA 23-5-706 covers casino night permits; it is not a self-exclusion statute. GCD regulates video gambling machines, card games, bingo, and charitable gaming but operates no statewide SE list. Access is property-by-property only. Confirmed by dojmt.gov and Kindbridge state-by-state review.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — Montana Lottery)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Montana Lottery's responsible gaming page (montanalottery.net/responsible/) contains tips and warning-sign information only; no self-exclusion program is offered or referenced. Montana has no statewide SE list for any gambling vertical. The Players Club is a loyalty program with no self-exclusion feature. Confirmed no SE program at Montana Lottery.

program_name: Nebraska Racing and Gaming Commission Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program (VSE)
enrollment_method: Online portal — choose duration, complete identity verification, digitally sign and submit
duration_options: 1 year; 3 years; 5 years; lifetime (lifetime only available after at least 6 months on list)
removal_process: Not described on public-facing site; contact NRGC
notes: NRGC state VSE program launched September 2022 ahead of casino opening. Covers all NRGC-licensed gaming establishments including racetrack casinos and pari-mutuel wagering. In December 2025 NRGC also activated NVSEP/idPair as an additional national option; state VSE program remains active. Helpline: 1-833-BETOVER.

program_name: Nebraska NVSEP (National Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program) via idPair
administering_body: Nebraska Racing and Gaming Commission (NRGC) / idPair
enrollment_method: Online at nvsep.org — identity verification; digital signature
duration_options: 1 year; 3 years; 5 years; 10 years; lifetime (per NVSEP platform)
notes: NVSEP activated for Nebraska by NRGC on December 22 2025 — Nebraska became part of the multi-state NVSEP network. Covers casinos and pari-mutuel wagering plus cross-state coverage for other NVSEP jurisdictions. Runs alongside the state VSE program.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — Nebraska Lottery)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Nebraska Lottery (administered by Nebraska Dept. of Revenue) offers no self-exclusion program. The Nebraska Racing and Gaming Commission (NRGC) joined NVSEP/idPair in December 2025; that program covers NRGC-regulated casinos and pari-mutuel wagering only. The Nebraska Lottery is a separate agency and is not regulated by NRGC and is not covered by the NVSEP activation. Nebraska Lottery Title 370 regulations (effective 9/21/2025) contain no self-exclusion provisions. Responsible gaming page refers to helpline only (833-BETOVER).

program_name: New Hampshire Lottery iLottery Self-Exclusion
enrollment_method: In-app/online via NH Lottery account settings
duration_options: 1 month; 3 months; 6 months; exclusion cannot be reversed until period expires
removal_process: Not described; automatically expires after selected period; contact NH Lottery support
notes: NH Lottery iLottery self-exclusion covers the NH Lottery online play platform and My603Rewards. Duration options are 1/3/6 months. Does not appear to cover in-store lottery purchases or charitable gaming venues.

program_name: New Hampshire Lottery Sports Betting (DraftKings) — Operator-Level Self-Exclusion
administering_body: DraftKings (exclusive NH Lottery sports betting partner)
enrollment_method: In-app via DraftKings account settings; DraftKings responsible gaming tools
notes: Sports betting in NH is operated exclusively by DraftKings under NH Lottery contract (deal extended February 2026). Self-exclusion is managed at operator level within the DraftKings app; no separate NH Lottery sports betting self-exclusion portal found.

program_name: NH Unified Self-Exclusion Database (authorized — implementation pending as of May 2026)
administering_body: New Hampshire Lottery & Gaming Commission (formerly NH Lottery Commission)
verticals_covered: all regulated gaming (VLTs;casino;historic horse racing;lottery;charitable gaming;sports wagering)
enrollment_method: Online platform (agent to be selected via competitive bid; RFI issued March 2026)
notes: HB 2 signed June 30 2025 authorizes NH Lottery & Gaming Commission to establish a centralized voluntary statewide self-exclusion database covering all legal gaming forms; agent to be selected via competitive bid approved by governor and executive council. As of May 2026: RFI (rfi-lottery-2026-02-0) issued March 2026 — procurement at RFI stage; no contract awarded. NH Council for Responsible Gaming launched April 2026 with no mention of database being live. No enrollment URL exists on nhlottery.com. Authorized but not yet operational; keep NOT FOUND until live enrollment confirmed.

program_name: New Mexico Gaming Control Board Self-Exclusion Program
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (racetrack casinos/racinos); Tribal gaming (all tribal gaming facilities)
enrollment_method: In person only — submit completed Self-Exclusion Request form at NMGCB office (4900 Alameda Blvd NE Albuquerque NM) or other approved location; must be photographed; government-issued photo ID required
removal_process: Written Petition for Removal from Self-Exclusion submitted to NMGCB; board reviews within 90 days; removal not available if less than 1 year since enrollment; must prove removal is in public interest
notes: Statewide program covers all racetrack casinos/racinos AND all tribal gaming facilities in NM — tribal inclusion is notable. Sports betting in NM is tribal-only so tribal self-exclusion coverage applies. NM Racing Commission does not operate a separate self-exclusion; NMGCB program encompasses race tracks.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program (confirmed — New Mexico Lottery)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: New Mexico Lottery has no self-exclusion program. The Healthy Play section (nmlottery.com/healthy-play/) offers responsible gaming tips and external counseling resources only — no SE enrollment. Jackpocket ceased NM operations (no iLottery as noted). The statewide NM Gaming Control Board self-exclusion covers racetracks/casinos/tribal facilities only (per rganm.org/self-exclusion/); lottery is not included. No account-based lottery play exists in NM to support an SE mechanism.

program_name: New York State Gaming Commission Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;VLT facilities;Horse/parimutuel racing (on- and off-track);Internet and account wagering;Sports betting (retail and mobile);New York Lottery (draw and instant);QuickDraw;Interactive fantasy sports;Charitable gaming
enrollment_method: In person at any licensed gaming facility's designated problem gambling intake area; or by U.S. mail to NYSGC headquarters (Schenectady) — form must be notarized
removal_process: At end of term, individual is automatically removed unless a new notarized form is submitted within 30 days; lifetime exclusion has no removal process
notes: Single statewide list covers all NYSGC-regulated verticals including sports betting (retail and mobile); online portal in development per legislation; no iLottery/online lottery product currently active. NY Council on Problem Gambling offers free VSE support including notary assistance.

program_name: National DFS self-exclusion via idPair / Coalition for Fantasy Sports — New York
administering_body: idPair / Coalition for Fantasy Sports operators (PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports)
enrollment_method: Free self-exclusion through participating DFS operators via the idPair/CFS platform
notes: New York is NOT a state-level NVSEP participant; the NYSGC runs its own statewide voluntary self-exclusion (separate row). idPair's footprint in NY is the March 2026 all-50-state DFS self-exclusion operated by the Coalition for Fantasy Sports (PrizePicks, Underdog, Dabble, Splash Sports), covering NY-licensed Interactive Fantasy Sports operators.

program_name: North Carolina Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program
verticals_covered: Sports betting (retail and online);Horse/parimutuel racing (advance deposit wagering)
enrollment_method: Online enrollment form on ncgaming.gov; also at NC Education Lottery Claims Centers; identity verification required
removal_process: Exclusion is irrevocable during the selected period; no reversal; removal upon expiration of non-lifetime term
notes: Approved Feb 2024; went live for online enrollment. Single form covers all NC licensed sports betting operators and licensed advance deposit horse race wagering operators. Program activated in advance of March 2024 sports betting launch. Takes effect within 3-4 business days.

program_name: North Carolina Education Lottery (NCEL) Online Play Self-Exclusion
verticals_covered: Lottery (online/iLottery account-based play; Lucke-Rewards; second-chance drawings)
enrollment_method: Online via NCEL account responsible gaming tools; or by calling NCEL Online Play Support Center (1-866-934-0289)
duration_options: Temporary (duration not specified in public sources); permanent
removal_process: Temporary exclusion ends at expiration; permanent exclusion cannot be reversed; contact support center for reinstatement after temporary period
notes: Covers NCEL account-based online play only; does not cover retail lottery ticket sales. Separate from ncgaming.gov sports betting self-exclusion. Specific duration options (months/years) not published on public FAQ — contact NCEL for details.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program for retail lottery (confirmed — NCEL retail scratch/draw)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: NCEL online account self-exclusion exists but is explicitly limited to NCEL Account access and Online Play Services (Lucke-Rewards second-chance drawings and online purchases). Official FAQ and Terms of Service confirm that self-exclusion does not exclude the player from retail scratch-off or draw ticket purchases at physical retailer locations. No separate retail VSE program exists. A separate sports betting VSE is administered by NC Gaming Commission (not NCEL).

program_name: North Dakota Lottery Pick & Click Online Account Self-Exclusion
verticals_covered: lottery (online draw games — Powerball;Mega Millions;Lotto America;Millionaire for Life;2by2)
enrollment_method: Online via account settings (Self Exclusion tab in player profile)
removal_process: Exclusion is irrevocable for the selected term; no early removal process described
notes: North Dakota Lottery operates Pick & Click — an online subscription draw-game system at club.lottery.nd.gov. Terms of Service and FAQ confirm account-level self-exclusion is available for 1 year / 5 years / 10 years / permanent via the Self Exclusion tab on the player profile page. ND Lottery is multi-state draw games only (no retail scratch VLT or iLottery beyond Pick & Click). Charitable gaming is separately regulated by the ND Attorney General Gaming Division; no self-exclusion program found for charitable gaming (pull-tabs bingo etc.) — that vertical remains NOT FOUND / needs human review.

program_name: North Dakota Tribal Casinos — Property-Level Self-Exclusion (no central state list)
administering_body: Individual tribal gaming commissions (Spirit Lake Gaming Commission; Standing Rock Sioux Tribe; Three Affiliated Tribes; Turtle Mountain Band; Sisseton-Wahpeton Oyate)
enrollment_method: In-person at each tribal property
notes: North Dakota is tribal gaming only; commercial casinos do not exist. The tribal-state compacts (reviewed via Governor's office and ND Legislative Council) do not require a centralized statewide self-exclusion list and do not expressly mandate operators to offer self-exclusion. Governor Doug Burgum executed new compacts with five tribal nations; reviewed text confirms no central state SE registry. Self-exclusion where available is property-level through individual tribal gaming commissions. AGA 2025 fact sheet corroborates no central state list.

program_name: Time Out Ohio Voluntary Exclusion Program
administering_body: Ohio Casino Control Commission (OCCC) and Ohio Lottery Commission (joint program)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;Racino/VLT facilities;Sports betting (retail and online/mobile);Fantasy contests/DFS (via OCCC-licensed operators)
enrollment_method: Online portal at TimeOutOhio.com (available 24/7); in person at any state casino or racino; or at OCCC Central Office (Columbus)
removal_process: Application for Removal after minimum period expires; convicted of criminal trespass if found on premises while enrolled
notes: Joint program of OCCC and Ohio Lottery Commission. Covers 4 casinos + 7 racinos + all sports gaming facilities + all online sportsbooks. DFS operators licensed by OCCC must prohibit enrolled individuals. Ohio confirmed as NVSEP/idPair participating jurisdiction (one of ~21 as of Dec 2025).

program_name: NVSEP (National Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program) — Ohio
verticals_covered: Sports betting (online — multi-state overlay);Casino (multi-state overlay where operators participate)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org
notes: Ohio confirmed as one of ~21 NVSEP participating jurisdictions as of late 2025. idPair operates NVSEP as a multi-state overlay. Ohio state program (Time Out Ohio) remains primary; NVSEP provides supplemental multi-state coverage.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program for live racing/ADW patrons (confirmed — Ohio State Racing Commission)
administering_body: Ohio State Racing Commission / individual ADW operators (TVG; FanDuel Racing; TwinSpires)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Time Out Ohio (the Ohio statewide VSE) explicitly covers Ohio casinos / 7 racinos / sports gaming only — ADW and live-track horse racing wagering are not listed as covered entities per official FAQ at timeoutohio.com. Ohio Administrative Code Title 3769 contains no patron self-exclusion requirement for horse racing licensees. Ohio Racing Commission regulations reviewed; no VSE provision found. SB 197 (2025) transfers OSRC functions to Ohio Casino Control Commission but does not create an ADW self-exclusion program. Individual ADW operators (TVG / TwinSpires) may offer account-level responsible gaming tools voluntarily but there is no state-mandated or state-administered ADW VSE program.

program_name: Oklahoma Lottery Self-Exclusion Program (SEP)
enrollment_method: Notarized SEP Agreement Form submitted to the Oklahoma Lottery; form available for download at lottery.ok.gov; must be notarized by third-party notary
duration_options: Lifetime only (irrevocable, no time-limited option confirmed)
removal_process: Lifetime ban — irrevocable; participant may not claim any lottery prize after enrollment
notes: Lifetime-only exclusion — distinct from casino/tribal programs which allow 1- to 10-year bans. Third parties cannot enroll on behalf of another person. Participants may no longer receive promotional communications from Oklahoma Lottery.

program_name: OAPGG Statewide Self-Exclusion Program (tribal casinos)
administering_body: Oklahoma Association on Problem Gambling and Gaming (OAPGG) — administered in partnership with tribal gaming commissions
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (tribal — 17 tribal gaming commissions; 94 participating casinos as of last report)
enrollment_method: Online form submission at oapgg.org; OAPGG staff schedules online notary session to confirm identity; form distributed to participating tribes
removal_process: Irrevocable during exclusion period; criminal trespass charges may apply if found on premises
notes: Not all tribes participate; 17 tribal commissions and ~94 casinos enrolled as of public reporting. Tribes may opt in at any time. Oklahoma has no commercial (non-tribal) casinos. Racinos are under OK Horse Racing Commission. SmartPlay OK is the companion responsible gambling brand (not a separate list).

program_name: SmartPlayOK Statewide Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program (includes racino facilities)
administering_body: Oklahoma Association on Problem Gambling and Gaming (OAPGG) — administered in coordination with participating tribal gaming commissions and non-tribal facilities including Remington Park
verticals_covered: horse/parimutuel racing;casino (HHR;slots;table games at racino facilities)
enrollment_method: Online (notarized electronic submission); by mail (notarized form); in-person at OAPGG Norman office (405-801-3329)
removal_process: Irrevocable for selected term; no early removal; contact OAPGG at help@oapgg.org
notes: OAPGG administers a statewide voluntary self-exclusion program via SmartPlayOK that covers participating gambling establishments including Remington Park Racing & Casino (which acquired Lone Star Park). Remington Park's official responsible gaming page explicitly references the statewide SmartPlayOK exclusion program and confirms its participation. Enrollment requires notarized identity verification. Exclusion results in denial of all winnings and potential trespassing charge. Oklahoma Horse Racing Commission regulations reviewed — no separate OHRC VSE framework found; racino patrons are covered by the OAPGG/SmartPlayOK program.

program_name: Oregon Lottery Scoreboard Self-Exclusion (online sports betting)
verticals_covered: Sports betting (online — Oregon Lottery Scoreboard app/website)
enrollment_method: Online via player account settings on Oregon Lottery Scoreboard; email to scoreboard.reinstate@support.oregonlottery.org for reinstatement
duration_options: Minimum 30 days; temporary (extendable indefinitely) or permanent
removal_process: Temporary: request reinstatement by emailing scoreboard.reinstate@support.oregonlottery.org after period expires; permanent exclusion is irrevocable
notes: Oregon Lottery runs the only authorized online sports betting in the state (via DraftKings-powered Scoreboard). Self-exclusion applies to the online player account only. Does NOT extend to retail video lottery terminals at bars/establishments. Oregon does not maintain a statewide problem-gambler ban list covering all verticals per 2026 reporting.

program_name: No voluntary self-exclusion program for VLT retail players (confirmed — Oregon Lottery VLT)
verticals_covered: video lottery terminals (VLT at ~12000 retail/bar establishments)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Oregon Administrative Rule OAR 177-046-0155 (Responsible Gaming) provides account-based self-exclusion — but OAR 177-046-0010 explicitly states Division 46 applies to all lottery games EXCEPT Video Lottery games. Division 200 (General Video Lottery Game Rules) contains no self-exclusion provisions. Oregon Lottery VLT play requires only cash insertion into retail terminals; no player account is required to play. Oregon Lottery acknowledges no centralized VLT ban list. IGT INTELLIGEN account-based system deployed (2022) for prize redemption only — no evidence that account-level self-exclusion extends to VLT play restriction at retail terminals per any official rule. Oregon Lottery VLT players have no state-administered self-exclusion mechanism.

program_name: Oregon Racing Commission ADW Self-Exclusion (horse racing account wagering)
verticals_covered: Horse/parimutuel racing (advance deposit wagering — licensed ADW operators)
enrollment_method: Through licensed ADW operator account settings (ORC rule requires ADW operators to offer self-exclusion)
removal_process: ORC rules require account closure/exclusion; account holder removed from all direct advertising; records retained minimum 3 years or duration of exclusion
