notes: ORC rules (effective July 1 2023) require all licensed ADW operators to offer self-exclusion. Self-exclusion administered by each licensed ADW operator per ORC mandate — not a single centralized ORC-run list. Operators must maintain records for duration of exclusion or 3 years minimum.

program_name: Oregon Tribal Casinos — Property-Level Self-Exclusion (no central state list)
administering_body: Individual tribal gaming commissions (Confederated Tribes of Grand Ronde; Confederated Tribes of Siletz Indians; Confederated Tribes of Warm Springs; Cow Creek Band; Coquille Indian Tribe; others)
enrollment_method: In-person at each tribal property
notes: Oregon is tribal casino only — no commercial casinos. Oregon Tribal Gaming Alliance (OTGA) website reviewed; no centralized tribal VSE program found. AGA 2025 Oregon fact sheet and independent sources confirm Oregon does not maintain a centralized statewide self-exclusion list and tribal casinos operate on a property-by-property basis. Each tribe/property may maintain its own internal exclusion list through its tribal gaming commission but there is no multi-tribal central registry in Oregon.

program_name: PGCB Casino Self-Exclusion Program
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (land-based);Sports betting (retail — at casino sportsbooks and freestanding sports wagering locations)
enrollment_method: Online at pgcb.pa.gov/SelfExclusion; in person at PGCB office or PGCB office located at a licensed facility
removal_process: Casino self-exclusion does NOT expire automatically — individual must submit Request for Removal after minimum period; remains on list indefinitely if no removal request
notes: Established 2006; covers 12 licensed land-based casinos and retail sports wagering locations. Separate from iGaming/Fantasy lists. Licensees may voluntarily extend bans across other gambling types. 2025: PGCB abandoned proposal to auto-expire casino exclusions.

program_name: PGCB iGaming Self-Exclusion Program
enrollment_method: Online at pgcb.pa.gov/SelfExclusion; in person at PGCB office or licensed facility
removal_process: iGaming self-exclusion expires automatically at end of selected period (unlike casino SE); extension must be submitted proactively
notes: Launched 2019. Covers all licensed PA online casino, online poker, and online sportsbook operators. Separate list from casino SE. Note: iGaming excludes include sports wagering websites per PGCB FAQ. Expires at conclusion of ban period — not permanent by default.

program_name: PGCB Fantasy Sports Self-Exclusion Program
verticals_covered: Fantasy contests/DFS (licensed PA fantasy sports operators)
enrollment_method: Online at pgcb.pa.gov/SelfExclusion; in person at PGCB office
removal_process: Fantasy SE expires automatically at end of selected period; extension must be submitted proactively
notes: Separate list from Casino and iGaming SE. Covers licensed PA fantasy contest operators. Auto-expires unlike Casino SE.

program_name: PGCB VGT Self-Exclusion Program
verticals_covered: Video gaming terminals (VGTs at licensed truck stops)
enrollment_method: Online at pgcb.pa.gov/SelfExclusion; in person at PGCB office
removal_process: VGT SE expires automatically at end of selected period; extension must be submitted proactively
notes: Separate list covering VGT facilities at licensed truck stops. Auto-expires unlike Casino SE.

program_name: PA iLottery Self-Exclusion
enrollment_method: Online via PA iLottery account settings (pailottery.com); or contact support at support@PAiLottery.com or 1-833-530-PLAY
removal_process: Irrevocable during selected period; after expiration player must contact support@PAiLottery.com to request reinstatement (not automatic)
notes: COMPLETELY SEPARATE from PGCB lists — being on PGCB casino/iGaming SE list does NOT prevent iLottery play, and vice versa. Covers online account-based lottery play only; does not affect retail lottery ticket purchases. Administered by PA Lottery not PGCB.

program_name: No state-administered self-exclusion for ADW horse racing (confirmed — PA State Horse Racing Commission)
administering_body: PA State Horse Racing Commission / individual ADW operators (TVG/FanDuel Racing; TwinSpires; NYRA Bets; others)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: PGCB self-exclusion list covers casino / iGaming / VGT / sports betting — ADW horse racing is explicitly absent from PGCB's regulated verticals. Reviewed 7 Pa. Code Chapter 197 (Advance Deposit Account Wagering — Temporary Regulations): §197.3 contains no self-exclusion provision; ADWs have discretion to refuse accounts but no mandatory VSE requirement. PA State Horse Racing Commission (now under Dept. of Agriculture) reviewed; no VSE framework exists for ADW patrons. Individual ADW operators (TwinSpires / TVG) may offer account-level responsible gaming tools voluntarily (TwinSpires FAQ references a permanent exclusion form via Player Services 877-774-7371) but these are operator-level only and not part of any state-administered program. No state-run or commission-mandated ADW self-exclusion program exists in Pennsylvania.

program_name: Rhode Island Self-Exclusion Program (Bally's / RI Lottery — Combined Form)
administering_body: Rhode Island Lottery (administering body); Dept. of Business Regulation (DBR) — Gaming and Athletics
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;Sports betting (retail and online/mobile);iGaming/online casino
enrollment_method: In person only — submit at Bally's Lincoln Casino Resort Security Dept. (100 Twin River Rd, Lincoln RI 02865) or Bally's Tiverton Security Dept. (777 Tiverton Casino Blvd, Tiverton RI 02878)
removal_process: Self-exclusion periods are served in their entirety; subject to termination with proper Bally's management/RI Lottery approvals
notes: Single combined form (updated Feb 2024) covers both Bally's Lincoln and Bally's Tiverton casino properties plus Sportsbook Rhode Island (online/mobile) and Bally Casino iGaming app — all RI Lottery-regulated gambling channels. In-person submission required at either casino security dept. PGSRI (401-383-0301) provides referral/enrollment assistance.

program_name: Rhode Island iLottery Self-Exclusion & Timeout
enrollment_method: Online via iLottery account responsible gambling tools; or by calling RI iLottery Player Support at 1-800-288-2485
duration_options: Self-Exclusion: 1 year or 5 years; Timeout: 24 hours / 48 hours / 7 days / 14 days / 1 month / 3 months / 6 months / 9 months
removal_process: Exclusion period expires automatically; account reactivated at end of period; exclusion cannot be reversed during active period
notes: Separate from the casino/sports/iGaming combined self-exclusion. Covers only online iLottery account play (not retail lottery tickets). Enrollment is online or by phone. Excludion cannot be reversed; at end of period account access is automatically restored.

program_name: No self-exclusion program for charitable gaming
administering_body: RI State Police Charitable Gaming Unit
enrollment_method: none
notes: RI State Police CGU regulates raffles/bingo/bazaars only; no SE program exists for charitable gaming. The statewide PGSRI SE program covers Bally's Twin River Lincoln and Tiverton casinos only — charitable gaming venues are excluded. CGU page has no RG/SE provisions; only age-18 restriction and fraud protection.

program_name: No self-exclusion program — SC Education Lottery
enrollment_method: none
notes: SC Education Lottery offers no voluntary self-exclusion program. Player protection page covers ticket verification and fraud prevention only. Play Responsibly section (BETTER U initiative) links to SC Gambling Helpline (1-877-452-5155) and NCPG but provides no SE enrollment. SC has no iLottery account layer. Retail-only lottery; no account-based tools.

program_name: South Dakota Commission on Gaming — Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program
administering_body: South Dakota Commission on Gaming (SD Dept. of Revenue)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (Deadwood);Sports betting (retail — Deadwood sportsbooks);Horse/parimutuel racing
enrollment_method: In person at a licensed gaming property or via South Dakota Commission on Gaming office; each licensee maintains plan approved by Commission executive secretary; centralized list shared with all licensees
duration_options: Not definitively confirmed in public sources; described as ranging from short-term to permanent (6 months to permanent reported in trade press)
removal_process: Not specified in public sources; contact Commission on Gaming at (605) 578-3074
notes: Governing reg: ARSD 20:18:12.01:26 (eff. 8/9/2022) — each licensee must develop and submit self-exclusion/voluntary exclusion plan for Commission approval with monthly reporting. Commission streamlined to one centralized statewide list (covering all Deadwood licensees) and removed requirement to visit a casino to apply. Sports betting in Deadwood (retail/on-premises only) and parimutuel racing (Stanley County Fairgrounds) also fall under Commission oversight. Tribal casinos are separate/property-level only.

program_name: No self-exclusion program — SD Lottery (video lottery)
enrollment_method: none
notes: SD Lottery Responsible Play page lists helpline (1-888-781-HELP) and treatment resources but no SE program. Video lottery (VLT terminals in bars/taverns) is regulated separately from Deadwood casinos. The Dec 2025 centralized SE rule change applies only to Deadwood (SD Commission on Gaming); SD Lottery/VLT has no SE program. Veridocs confirms SD has no centralized VLT self-exclusion list.

program_name: Property-by-property self-exclusion — tribal casinos
administering_body: Individual tribal gaming commissions (9 SD tribes)
enrollment_method: in-person at each tribal property
notes: AGA 2025 SD Regulatory Fact Sheet confirms tribal-state compacts do not require SE programs for patrons. Nine tribes operate Class III gaming under compacts; each tribal gaming commission is responsible for its own responsible gaming policies. No state-mandated or shared SE list exists for tribal casinos. Individual tribes may maintain their own property-level lists.

program_name: Tennessee Sports Wagering Self-Exclusion (TSWAC)
administering_body: Tennessee Sports Wagering Advisory Council (TSWAC / SWAC)
verticals_covered: Sports betting (online — all licensed TN sports wagering operators)
enrollment_method: Online via tn.gov/swac/forms/self-exclusion.html; by phone at (615) 253-7922; in person at any licensed TN sportsbook
removal_process: Not specified in public sources; contact TSWAC at (615) 253-7922
notes: Statewide program covering all TSWAC-licensed online sports wagering operators. A single self-exclusion prevents wagering on all licensed TN sportsbook apps; excluded persons also removed from direct marketing lists. Tennessee is online-only for sports betting (no retail sportsbooks outside of Deadwood-equivalent). Program predates NVSEP.

program_name: NVSEP (National Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program) — Tennessee
verticals_covered: Sports betting (online); possibly additional verticals per nvsep.org/jurisdictions
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org; free remote online notarization provided where required
notes: Tennessee was one of 4 launch states for NVSEP in August 2024 (with CO, IA, MI). NVSEP is operated by idPair Inc. and provides free self-exclusion assistance overlaying the existing TSWAC state program. No changes to the state program; NVSEP assists with form filing and remote notarization. Specific TN verticals: see nvsep.org/jurisdictions. As of Dec 2025 NVSEP covers ~21 jurisdictions.

program_name: Tennessee Education Lottery — iLottery Self-Exclusion
enrollment_method: Online via iLottery account settings (temporary exclusion and deposit limits); or contact TN iLottery Player Support
duration_options: Temporary (account block for a set period chosen by player); irrevocable during the period
removal_process: Temporary period expires; account remains blocked until period ends; cannot be reversed mid-period
notes: TN Lottery iLottery launched Nov 2024. Lottery self-exclusion (online account tool) is separate from TSWAC sports wagering exclusion. Covers iLottery online play only — not retail lottery ticket sales. Program achieved WLA Level 4 Responsible Gaming certification. Specific duration options not publicly published (described as user-set period).

program_name: No self-exclusion program — TN Lottery retail + TN charitable gaming
administering_body: Tennessee Education Lottery Corporation (retail); TN Secretary of State (charitable gaming)
verticals_covered: lottery (retail draw/scratch); charitable gaming (annual events — raffles; bingo; reverse raffles)
enrollment_method: none
notes: TN Lottery SE exists only for iLottery online account (a separate existing row). Retail lottery (scratch-off; draw games at retail) has no SE mechanism — no account required. TN charitable gaming (annual events authorized by General Assembly; raffles/bingo/reverse raffles regulated by TN Secretary of State) has no SE program; SOS website covers eligibility/reporting only. No self-exclusion provisions in TN Secretary of State charitable gaming rules.

program_name: No self-exclusion program — Texas Lottery (TDLR)
administering_body: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation — Lottery Division
enrollment_method: none
notes: Texas Lottery (transferred to TDLR Sept 1 2025) has no voluntary SE program. Player Protection page covers age verification; payment restrictions; and scam education. Responsible gambling page emphasizes education/helpline only. 2025 TDLR reforms added ID scanning on vending machines and lowered cash limits but no SE mechanism. No iLottery account system exists for retail.

program_name: Property-level only — Texas horse/parimutuel racing
enrollment_method: in-person at each track security department
notes: TXRC maintains Ejection/Exclusion List (November 2025 notice on rulings page) — this is involuntary/regulatory exclusion not a patron self-exclusion program. Lone Star Park offers a property-level voluntary self-exclusion form through Security Supervisory staff. No state-run patron voluntary SE program exists. TX ended ADW/online wagering (TwinSpires ordered to cease Feb 2026); only on-track betting active. No centralized voluntary SE program.

program_name: No self-exclusion program — Texas charitable bingo (TDLR)
administering_body: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation — Charitable Bingo Operations Division
enrollment_method: none
notes: TDLR Charitable Bingo Operations Division website has no SE program; RG reference links only to general Texas Lottery responsible gambling page. No SE provisions in Chapter 402 Charitable Bingo Administrative Rules. Charitable bingo SE programs are extremely rare nationally; none found for Texas.

program_name: Utah — No legal gambling
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: All forms of gambling prohibited under Utah Code Title 76 Ch. 10 Part 11. No gaming regulatory body. No self-exclusion program.

program_name: Vermont Exclusion List (Sports Wagering)
administering_body: Vermont Department of Liquor and Lottery (DLL)
verticals_covered: Sports betting (online — all licensed VT sports wagering operators: DraftKings / FanDuel / Fanatics Sportsbook)
enrollment_method: Online enrollment via Vermont DLL; requires email/phone for one-time authentication code and photo ID (selfie + photo ID upload)
removal_process: Automatic removal at end of selected period (non-lifetime); lifetime exclusion has no removal process; re-enrollment required to continue after term expires
notes: Authorized by Act 63 (2023). Managed by DLL; exclusion list kept confidential under VT Public Records Act. Covers all 3 licensed online sportsbooks. Enrolled persons removed from marketing lists. Contact: DLL.PlayerExclusion@vermont.gov.

program_name: Vermont Lottery — Online account self-exclusion
administering_body: Vermont Department of Liquor and Lottery (DLL) — Vermont Lottery division
enrollment_method: Online via Vermont Lottery account settings (spending/time limits; self-exclusion tools)
duration_options: Not definitively confirmed in public sources (account-based self-exclusion available; duration options unclear)
notes: Vermont Lottery digital modernization project in progress as of Jan 2026. Lottery self-exclusion tools are available through account settings per responsible gaming program. DLL oversees both sports wagering and lottery; DLL Exclusion List may also cover online lottery products — not fully confirmed as separate or unified list. Charitable gaming and DFS have no separate self-exclusion finding.

program_name: CFS/idPair National Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program — DFS (covers VT); no SE for charitable gaming
administering_body: Coalition for Fantasy Sports (CFS) and idPair (technology platform)
enrollment_method: online via individual operator platform (PrizePicks; Underdog; Betr; Dabble; Splash Sports)
removal_process: player contacts operator platform to remove or modify
notes: CFS/idPair National Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program expanded to all 50 states as of March 10 2026 — Vermont is covered. Participating DFS operators: PrizePicks; Underdog; Betr; Dabble; Splash Sports. DFS operators register with VT Dept of Liquor and Lottery (sports wagering division); AG handles enforcement. idpair_flag=yes (this is the CFS/idPair all-50 operator-led program). VT charitable gaming (raffles; bingo; break-open tickets regulated by Dept of Liquor and Lottery) has no SE program — Vermont statute 13 V.S.A. s.2143 contains no SE provisions; no state-level license even required for most charitable gaming events.

program_name: Virginia Lottery Voluntary Exclusion Program (VEP)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;Sports betting (online);Lottery (account-based/iLottery);Charitable gaming (raffle;bingo;network bingo;instant bingo);Horse/parimutuel racing (live;OTB;HHR;advance deposit wagering);DFS/Fantasy Contests
enrollment_method: Online form for 2-year or 5-year; in-person at Lottery HQ or casino location (call 804-692-7185) for lifetime
removal_process: Lottery auto-removes at expiration of period; early removal not permitted; extension available before expiration
notes: Single statewide VEP administered by VA Lottery covers all verticals: casino gaming; sports betting; account-based lottery; charitable gaming (OCRP-regulated); horse racing and HHR (VRC-regulated); and DFS/fantasy contests (OCRP-regulated). Within 7 days of enrollment Lottery distributes info to all casinos; sports betting permit holders; OCRP; and VRC. Lifetime exclusion requires in-person appointment. Forfeiture of winnings to Problem Gambling Treatment and Support Fund. Regulated under 11VAC5-60.

program_name: NVSEP / idPair (DFS vertical - national 50-state program)
administering_body: idPair / Coalition for Fantasy Sports (PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org or participating DFS operator platform
notes: March 2026: idPair and DFS operators (PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports) launched 50-state NVSEP DFS self-exclusion covering all U.S. jurisdictions including Virginia. Separate from state VEP; covers DFS vertical across all participating operators via single portal. Virginia DFS also covered by state VEP administered by OCRP.

program_name: Washington State Gambling Commission Statewide Self-Exclusion Program (WSGC SEP)
enrollment_method: Online or in-person enrollment form (GC4-034); mail with notarization or in-person at WSGC
duration_options: Not publicly specified in search results; form-based selection
removal_process: Cannot exit early; must submit notarized removal form after period expires; 45-day advance notice sent before expiration
notes: Launched May 2022 following HB 1302 (2019). Covers all privately-owned house-banked card rooms statewide via single form. Tribal casinos NOT currently participating in WSGC statewide program — tribal self-exclusion is property/tribe-level. No statewide sports betting program (all WA sports betting is tribal on-premises only). No charitable gaming self-exclusion program through WSGC identified.

program_name: Washington's Lottery Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program
enrollment_method: Print and mail (notarized) or in-person at a Lottery office (no notarization required in person)
duration_options: One year; three years; five years
removal_process: Cannot exit early; submit notarized removal form after period expires; 45-day advance notice before expiration
notes: Covers lottery play (retail and account-based). Enrolled persons prohibited from collecting prizes over $600 or any second-chance drawing prize. Prize forfeitures go to state problem gambling account. Separate from WSGC card room program. No WA Lottery NVSEP participation found.

program_name: Washington Tribal Casinos — Property-by-Property Self-Exclusion
verticals_covered: Tribal casino gaming + tribal on-premises sports betting
enrollment_method: Per-property (contact each tribal casino directly)
notes: WSGC explicitly states tribal casinos are NOT participating in the WSGC statewide program (which covers only house-banked card rooms). WSGC advises contacting each tribal casino directly about its own SE program. All 29 federally recognized tribes in WA run property-level programs. No central tribal list exists.

program_name: NVSEP / idPair (DFS vertical - national 50-state program)
administering_body: idPair / Coalition for Fantasy Sports (PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org or participating DFS operator platform
notes: March 2026: idPair 50-state DFS NVSEP covers Washington for DFS vertical via PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports. WA also has active NVSEP page at nvsep.org (confirmed from earlier searches showing nvsep.org state-specific pages). Separate from WSGC card room program.

program_name: No Statewide Horse Racing / ADW Self-Exclusion Program — WHRC
enrollment_method: Not available — no WHRC central program found
notes: No patron self-exclusion program found on the WHRC website (whrc.wa.gov). WSGC statewide SE covers only cardrooms, not racing. Licensed ADW operators (TVG, TwinSpires, NYRA Bets, etc.) operate account-level responsible gaming tools at the operator level, but WHRC has no central patron SE list. Human review recommended to confirm absence or check WAC 260-49 ADW rules for any embedded SE requirement.

program_name: West Virginia Lottery Self-Exclusion Program (casino; iGaming; sports betting)
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (racetrack casinos + Greenbrier);iGaming/online casino;Sports betting (online/retail)
enrollment_method: Notarized paper form mailed to WV Lottery Commission
duration_options: Indefinite (minimum 1 year before removal request eligible)
removal_process: Request for removal after minimum 1-year period; Lottery reviews request
notes: Covers all four WV racetrack casinos (Mardi Gras; The Casino Club at The Greenbrier; Mountaineer; Wheeling Island) plus iGaming and sports betting. DOES NOT cover iLottery (iPlay) — that is a separate process. Form requires notarization; mail to WV Lottery. Personal description (tattoos; birthmarks; SSN; DL) required. Winnings forfeited; criminal trespass if caught at property.

program_name: West Virginia Lottery iPlay Self-Exclusion (iLottery)
enrollment_method: Online via iPlay account responsible gaming tools OR call iPlay Support Center 1-833-376-0384
removal_process: Permanent exclusion is irreversible; temporary exclusion ends after selected period; cannot re-enter during exclusion
notes: Separate and distinct from casino/iGaming/sports betting self-exclusion. Covers only iPlay (iLottery) account-based play. During exclusion: cannot log in; cannot use PlayOn; cannot enter second-chance drawings or promotions. Note: self-excluding from casinos/iGaming/sports betting does NOT auto-exclude from iPlay — player must enroll in both programs separately.

program_name: West Virginia Lottery Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program
verticals_covered: Horse racing (all 4 WV Racetracks), video lottery terminals at racetracks, casino gaming, iGaming, sports betting
enrollment_method: Online form submission or mail to WV Lottery Deputy Director of Casinos
removal_process: Request to Deputy Director of Casinos; minimum 1 year before removal request eligible
notes: WV Lottery SE explicitly covers 'all four West Virginia Racetracks and Casinos as well as The Historic Greenbrier Casino' plus mobile/online gaming. WV Racing Commission (racing.wv.gov) has no separate patron SE program; all gaming venues including racetracks fall under WV Lottery SE administration. The WV Lottery is the single SE administrator for all WV gaming verticals including horse racing venues.

program_name: NVSEP / idPair (West Virginia — gaming/sports betting)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org
notes: NVSEP has a dedicated West Virginia page (nvsep.org/west-virginia) confirming WV is a participating jurisdiction. NVSEP provides self-exclusion assistance alongside (not replacing) the state WV Lottery program. Covers gaming/casino/sports betting verticals. WV was among early NVSEP states. DFS additionally covered by March 2026 50-state DFS NVSEP expansion.

program_name: NVSEP / idPair (DFS vertical - national 50-state program)
administering_body: idPair / Coalition for Fantasy Sports (PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org or participating DFS operator platform
notes: March 2026 50-state DFS NVSEP expansion covers West Virginia DFS via PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports.

program_name: No Self-Exclusion Program — Wisconsin Lottery (retail only)
verticals_covered: Lottery (retail draw and instant games only; no iLottery)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Wisconsin Lottery responsible gaming page (wilottery.com/resp-gaming/home) provides problem gambling resources and helpline referrals but contains no self-exclusion program. Wisconsin Lottery has no online/iLottery play; retail-only operation. No SE mechanism found on official site.

program_name: Wisconsin Tribal Casinos — Property-by-Property Self-Exclusion (Potawatomi joined TWSEP/idPair 2025)
administering_body: Individual Tribal Gaming Commissions (Potawatomi also via TWSEP/idPair)
verticals_covered: Tribal casino gaming + tribal on-premises sports betting
enrollment_method: Per-property (contact each tribal casino directly); Potawatomi enrolls via property or TWSEP
notes: No statewide WI tribal SE list. WI DOA Office of Indian Gaming has no central list. Each tribe administers own SE program. Potawatomi Casino Hotel (Milwaukee) became first WI tribe to join Tribal-Wide Self-Exclusion Program (TWSEP) powered by idPair in March 2025 — TWSEP is part of NVSEP. idpair_flag=yes for Potawatomi/TWSEP. Other WI tribes remain property-only.

program_name: NVSEP / idPair — Tribal-Wide Self-Exclusion Program (TWSEP) via Potawatomi and idPair
administering_body: idPair / Tribal Council for Responsible Gaming; Potawatomi Casino Hotel (Milwaukee; first WI participant)
enrollment_method: In-person at Potawatomi Casino Hotel (414-847-7608) or online via idPair/NVSEP
notes: March 2025: Potawatomi Casino Hotel (Milwaukee) became first Wisconsin tribal casino in TWSEP (Tribal-Wide Self-Exclusion Program) — part of NVSEP operated by idPair. Covers Potawatomi and any other casinos joining TWSEP nationally. Wisconsin Council on Problem Gambling co-partner. Other WI tribes not confirmed in program as of research date. DFS vertical additionally covered by March 2026 50-state NVSEP DFS expansion.

program_name: NVSEP / idPair (DFS vertical - national 50-state program)
administering_body: idPair / Coalition for Fantasy Sports (PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org or participating DFS operator platform
notes: March 2026 50-state DFS NVSEP expansion covers Wisconsin DFS via PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports.

program_name: No Self-Exclusion Program — Wisconsin Charitable Gaming (bingo/raffles)
administering_body: Wisconsin DOA Office of Charitable Gaming
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Wisconsin DOA Office of Charitable Gaming (doa.wi.gov) regulates bingo and raffles. No self-exclusion program exists for charitable gaming in Wisconsin. Consistent with national norm — charitable bingo/raffle SE is virtually non-existent.

program_name: Wyoming Gaming Commission Self-Exclusion Program (NVSEP-integrated)
verticals_covered: Sports betting (online);Horse/parimutuel racing (incl. HHR);Skill-based amusement games
enrollment_method: Online via Wyoming Gaming Commission website (gaming.wyo.gov) or nvsep.org
duration_options: One year; three years; five years; ten years; lifetime
notes: December 8 2025: Wyoming Gaming Commission voted to join NVSEP (21st U.S. jurisdiction). WGC directs enrollees to NVSEP portal. Covers sports wagering; pari-mutuel wagering; HHR; skill-based amusement games regulated by WGC. Exclusion shared with all WGC-permitted operators. Duration options: 1; 3; 5; 10 years or lifetime. idPair technology. WyoLotto (lottery) appears separate — no formal WyoLotto self-exclusion program confirmed.

program_name: NVSEP / idPair (Wyoming — sports betting; racing; skill games via WGC)
verticals_covered: Sports betting (online);Horse/parimutuel racing (incl. HHR);Skill-based amusement games
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org or gaming.wyo.gov
duration_options: One year; three years; five years; ten years; lifetime
notes: Wyoming is the 21st NVSEP jurisdiction (joined Dec 8 2025). WGC self-exclusion program IS the NVSEP program for WY — administered jointly. Program covers WGC-regulated products: online sports wagering; pari-mutuel; HHR. Massachusetts announced as next (22nd) jurisdiction.

program_name: No Self-Exclusion Program — WyoLotto (retail only)
verticals_covered: Lottery (retail draw and instant games only; no iLottery/online play)
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: WyoLotto responsible gambling page provides problem gambling resources and hotline but no self-exclusion program. WyoLotto has no online play (retail tickets only). No SE mechanism found. Wyoming's NVSEP program (via Wyoming Gaming Commission) covers sports wagering and parimutuel but is administered by WGC, not WyoLotto.

program_name: Wyoming Charitable Gaming — Not Covered by WGC NVSEP Self-Exclusion
administering_body: Wyoming Gaming Commission (charitable gaming licensing only)
verticals_covered: Charitable gaming (bingo/pull-tabs) — not within SE scope
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: WGC NVSEP self-exclusion program (gaming.wyo.gov/resources/responsible-gaming/self-exclusion) explicitly covers online sports wagering, pari-mutuel wagering, and skill-based amusement games. Charitable gaming (bingo/pull-tabs) regulated by WGC but NOT within the SE program scope. No SE program exists for charitable gaming. Per WGC language: 'SE program is established for the purpose of allowing persons who wish to refrain from sports wagering and other types of gambling regulated by the Commission' — charitable gaming venues don't have account-based wagering and are not part of NVSEP coverage.

program_name: NVSEP / idPair (DFS vertical - national 50-state program)
administering_body: idPair / Coalition for Fantasy Sports (PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org or participating DFS operator platform
notes: March 2026 50-state DFS NVSEP expansion covers Wyoming DFS via PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports.

program_name: DC Office of Lottery and Gaming Self-Exclusion Program
administering_body: DC Office of Lottery and Gaming (OLG)
verticals_covered: Lottery (retail and iLottery);Sports betting (mobile and retail/Class A & B facilities)
enrollment_method: In-person at Class A or B sports wagering facility or OLG HQ (2235 Shannon Place SE; DC 20020); contact Responsible Gambling Specialist at 202-788-2102
duration_options: Minimum one (1) year; lifetime (lifetime only available after at least 6 months on list)
removal_process: Irrevocable during term; term may be increased but not reduced; enrollment process requires in-person signed application
notes: Program established per D.C. Code §§ 36-601 et seq. Single program covers all OLG-regulated gambling: sports wagering (Class A & B licensees; mobile wagering) AND lottery (retail ticket purchases and iLottery). Self-exclusion from sports betting automatically includes DC Lottery. Exclusion covers all licensed facilities; retail locations; and mobile wagering platforms DC-wide. Contact: Donna White (Responsible Gambling Specialist) 202-788-2102 or olgresponsiblegaming@dc.gov.

program_name: NVSEP / idPair (DFS vertical - national 50-state program)
administering_body: idPair / Coalition for Fantasy Sports (PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports)
enrollment_method: Online via nvsep.org or participating DFS operator platform
notes: March 2026 50-state DFS NVSEP expansion covers DC DFS via PrizePicks; Underdog; Dabble; Splash Sports. DC charitable gaming (raffles; bingo; Monte Carlo nights licensed by OLG) — no dedicated self-exclusion program found for charitable gaming specifically; DC OLG program covers lottery and sports wagering.

program_name: Autoexclusión de Juegos de Azar (Self-Exclusion from Gambling)
administering_body: Comisión de Juegos del Gobierno de Puerto Rico
verticals_covered: Commercial casino;Horse/parimutuel racing;Sports betting;DFS/Fantasy contests;Route gaming machines
enrollment_method: Online via comjuegos.pr.gov and in-person at licensed establishments
removal_process: Affidavit filed with Commission plus medical certification from certified mental health professional demonstrating absence of gambling behavior disorder
notes: Unified program under Act 81-2019 (as amended) covers casino (Negociado de Juegos de Azar), horse racing (Negociado del Deporte Hipico), sports betting/fantasy/route machines (Negociado de Apuestas Deportivas y Concurso de Fantasia). Enrollment email: autoexclusion@comjuegos.pr.gov. PAS helpline 1-800-981-0023.

program_name: No dedicated lottery self-exclusion (confirmed); responsible-gaming resources only
administering_body: Loterías de Puerto Rico (Dept. of Treasury/Hacienda); ASSMCA PAJC for treatment
enrollment_method: No self-exclusion enrollment for the lottery; "Juego Responsable" pages provide the helpline 1-800-981-0023 and ASSMCA's Programa de Ayuda al Jugador Compulsivo (PAJC, Ley 74-2006)
notes: The Hacienda-run traditional and electronic lottery offers responsible-gaming resources (helpline + ASSMCA PAJC treatment, which explicitly covers lottery players) but NO lottery-specific self-exclusion enrollment. Puerto Rico's voluntary self-exclusion (autoexclusión) is administered by the Comisión de Juegos and applies to casino, horse racing, and sports betting (captured in a separate row).

program_name: Voluntary Exclusion from Casino Gambling
enrollment_method: In-person only at VICCC office (3005 Orange Grove Christiansted St. Croix) during business hours Mon-Fri 8am-5pm
removal_process: Not specified; exclusion is for the chosen period with no early removal process described
notes: Program administered through PlayWiseVI.org — the VICCC's multilingual responsible gaming website. Participants barred from gaming floors, comps, players club, casino credit, check cashing. Criminal misdemeanor prosecution possible if patron found on casino floor during exclusion period. Limited to St. Croix casinos only.

program_name: No self-exclusion program found — iGaming not yet implemented
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: IGG Act authorizing internet gambling passed in 2001 but has remained dormant and unimplemented as of 2026. No online casino is operational and no self-exclusion program exists for this vertical.

program_name: No self-exclusion program (confirmed) — VI Lottery
enrollment_method: No self-exclusion; the official Play Responsibly page refers players only to the National Council on Problem Gambling (800-522-4700)
notes: VI Lottery's official responsible-gaming page provides only NCPG/helpline referral with no self-exclusion enrollment program. (Casino self-exclusion is run separately by the VI Casino Control Commission via PlayWiseVI.)

program_name: No horse-racing self-exclusion (confirmed); not covered by casino VEP
enrollment_method: No racing-specific self-exclusion; the VICCC Voluntary Exclusion (PlayWiseVI) covers "all gaming activities at USVI licensed casinos" only
notes: USVI horse racing has faced prolonged operational disruption. No self-exclusion program for parimutuel racing was found; the territory's only voluntary self-exclusion program (VICCC PlayWiseVI) is limited to licensed casinos.

program_name: No self-exclusion program — limited legal gambling
administering_body: Guam Department of Revenue and Taxation
enrollment_method: Not applicable — no responsible-gaming/self-exclusion framework identified
notes: Gambling on Guam is largely prohibited with limited exceptions (charitable bingo/raffles for nonprofits; a lottery via the Dept. of Revenue and Taxation). No official responsible-gaming or self-exclusion program could be located; needs human review.

program_name: No confirmed self-exclusion program — Commonwealth Casino Commission
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (Class III — single integrated resort, Saipan)
enrollment_method: Not determinable — sole casino non-operational
notes: CCC statute mandates responsible-gaming programs, but no self-exclusion program could be confirmed and the sole casino (Imperial Pacific, Saipan) has been closed since 2020. A pending bill / EO 2025-002 may dissolve the CCC and move authority to the Commonwealth Lottery Commission. Needs human review.

program_name: No self-exclusion program found — Commonwealth Lottery Commission
enrollment_method: Not applicable — none identified
notes: No self-exclusion program for the CNMI lottery was located via official sources. The Commonwealth Lottery Commission may absorb CCC functions per EO 2025-002. Needs human review.

program_name: No self-exclusion program — gambling prohibited
enrollment_method: N/A
notes: Gambling is prohibited in American Samoa under territorial law. The only exception is occasional bingo and raffles for charitable/religious/educational purposes (AS Code 46.4302). No lottery, commercial casino, parimutuel racing, or sports betting authorized. No self-exclusion program exists or is applicable.

program_name: Tribal Self-Exclusion Programs (IGRA — Class II/III Gaming)
administering_body: Tribal gaming commissions (individual tribes); National Indian Gaming Commission (NIGC) provides federal oversight but does NOT operate a national list
verticals_covered: Commercial casino (tribal Class II gaming;tribal Class III gaming)
enrollment_method: Varies by tribe — each tribal gaming commission sets its own program enrollment process
notes: NIGC does NOT operate a national tribal self-exclusion list. Self-exclusion is administered property-by-property or tribe-by-tribe under individual Tribal Gaming Commission ordinances approved by NIGC under IGRA (25 U.S.C. § 2710). NIGC Office of Self-Regulation relates to tribal self-governance of Class II operations, not patron exclusion lists. Some tribes participate in state lists or NVSEP voluntarily. No centralized federal list exists. 520+ tribal gaming establishments across 29 states.
